name: MiroFish GTM Demo — Production Polish
description: Transform the working prototype into a presentation-ready product with 92 polish tasks across infrastructure, LLM integration, visualizations, UX, testing, and documentation.

tasks.title: Production Dockerfile with gunicorn + multi-stage build
tasks.description: Replace the current single-stage backend/Dockerfile (which uses `python demo_app.py` directly) with a production-ready multi-stage build. Stage 1 (builder): use node:20-slim to build the frontend from frontend/ — copy package.json and pnpm-lock.yaml, run `corepack enable && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, copy source, run `pnpm run build`. Stage 2 (runtime): use python:3.11-slim, install gunicorn via pip alongside existing Flask dependencies, copy backend/ source and the built frontend dist/ into a static directory. Add a non-root user (`useradd -r appuser`), a HEALTHCHECK instruction hitting /api/health, and optimize layer caching by copying requirements before source. The CMD should run `gunicorn -w 4 -b 0.0.0.0:5001 demo_app:app`. Create this as a root-level Dockerfile replacing the two separate ones.

tasks.title: /api/health/detailed endpoint with LLM/dependency status
tasks.description: Add a new GET endpoint `/api/health/detailed` in backend/demo_app.py that returns a JSON object with comprehensive health information. Check: Flask app status (always ok), LLM provider connectivity (attempt a minimal API call to the configured provider using llm_client.py — catch exceptions and report status/error), Zep connection status (if ZEP_API_KEY is set, test the connection), current memory usage via `resource.getrusage` or `psutil`, server uptime (track app start time in a module-level variable), and version info read from frontend/package.json if available. Return a top-level `status` field that is 'healthy' only if all critical checks pass, 'degraded' if optional services are down, or 'unhealthy' if Flask itself has issues. Include a `checks` object with individual service statuses.

tasks.title: Environment validation on startup with error messages
tasks.description: Add a startup validation function in backend/demo_app.py that runs before the Flask app begins serving requests. Define required env vars (none currently — the app works without LLM keys) and optional env vars (LLM_PROVIDER, LLM_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL_NAME, ZEP_API_KEY, DEMO_SPEED, ALLOWED_ORIGINS, LOG_FILE, PORT). For each optional var, log a clear INFO message like 'LLM_API_KEY not set — LLM features will use fallback keyword matching'. For invalid configurations (e.g., LLM_PROVIDER set to an unsupported value, or LLM_PROVIDER set but LLM_API_KEY missing), log a WARNING with specific remediation steps. Call this function at module level after `load_dotenv()` but before route registration. Use Python's logging module with a structured format.

tasks.title: CORS configuration with explicit origin allowlist
tasks.description: Replace the current wildcard `CORS(app)` call in backend/demo_app.py with an explicit origin allowlist. Read the ALLOWED_ORIGINS environment variable (comma-separated string, e.g., 'http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:3000,https://gtm-mirofish.up.railway.app'). If ALLOWED_ORIGINS is not set, default to ['http://localhost:5173', 'http://localhost:3000'] for development. Parse the comma-separated string, strip whitespace, and pass to `CORS(app, origins=allowed_origins)`. Also set `supports_credentials=True` for auth cookie support. Log the configured origins at startup for debugging.

tasks.title: docker-compose.production.yml with gunicorn + nginx
tasks.description: Create a new docker-compose.production.yml file at the project root alongside the existing docker-compose.yml. Define three services: 1) `nginx` — use nginx:alpine image, mount a custom nginx.conf that proxies /api/* to the backend, serves frontend static files from a shared volume, and includes SSL-ready configuration (commented out Let's Encrypt blocks). 2) `backend` — build from the new production Dockerfile, run with gunicorn (4 workers, 120s timeout), expose only to the internal Docker network (not host), include health check, restart: always, and resource limits. 3) Define a shared network and named volumes for static files and simulation data. Include environment variable passthrough for all LLM/Zep/auth keys. Add a `nginx/nginx.conf` file with gzip compression, proxy headers (X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For), and static file caching headers.

tasks.title: Streaming chat via SSE (/api/report/chat/stream)
tasks.description: Add a new POST endpoint `/api/report/chat/stream` in backend/demo_app.py that returns Server-Sent Events for streaming LLM responses. Use Flask's `Response` class with `mimetype='text/event-stream'` and a Python generator function. The generator should: send an initial `event: start` with metadata, then for each token/chunk from the LLM, send `data: {"token": "..."}` as an SSE event. Add streaming support to backend/llm_client.py by creating a `chat_completion_stream()` function that uses the Anthropic `client.messages.stream()` and OpenAI `stream=True` parameter. On error, send `event: error` with the error message. On completion, send `event: done`. If no LLM key is configured, fall back to simulating streaming by yielding the existing keyword-match response word-by-word with small delays.

tasks.title: Gemini provider in llm_client.py
tasks.description: Add Google Gemini as a third LLM provider in backend/llm_client.py alongside the existing Anthropic and OpenAI implementations. Add a new entry in `_DEFAULTS`: `'gemini': {'model': 'gemini-1.5-flash'}`. Add a `_call_gemini()` function that imports `google.generativeai`, configures it with the API key, creates a `GenerativeModel` instance, and calls `generate_content()`. Handle the message format conversion: extract system messages as a system_instruction parameter, convert remaining messages to Gemini's Content format. Support both 'gemini-1.5-flash' and 'gemini-1.5-pro' models. Add 'gemini' to the provider check in `chat_completion()`. Also add Gemini streaming support in the new `chat_completion_stream()` function using `generate_content(stream=True)`. Add `google-generativeai` to the pip install in the Dockerfile.

tasks.title: LLM-powered seed text generation endpoint
tasks.description: Add a POST endpoint `/api/scenario/generate-seed` in backend/demo_app.py that uses the configured LLM to generate creative seed text for simulation scenarios. Accept JSON body with fields: `topic` (string, required), `industry` (string, optional), `persona_types` (list of strings, optional), and `tone` (string, optional — 'professional', 'provocative', 'analytical'). Construct a system prompt that instructs the LLM to generate a 200-400 word seed document suitable for a GTM simulation, incorporating the specified industry context and persona perspectives. Call `chat_completion()` from llm_client.py. Return structured JSON: `{seed_text: string, metadata: {topic, industry, word_count, generated_at}}`. If no LLM is configured, return a pre-built seed text from the matching GTM scenario template in backend/gtm_scenarios/. Include input validation for required fields.

tasks.title: Token usage tracking + /api/settings/llm-usage
tasks.description: Add token usage tracking to backend/llm_client.py by modifying `_call_anthropic()` and `_call_openai()` to extract and record token counts from API responses. Anthropic responses have `response.usage.input_tokens` and `response.usage.output_tokens`; OpenAI has `response.usage.prompt_tokens` and `response.usage.completion_tokens`. Store cumulative usage in a module-level dictionary: `_usage = {'anthropic': {'input': 0, 'output': 0, 'requests': 0}, 'openai': {...}, 'gemini': {...}, 'by_endpoint': {}}`. Track per-endpoint usage by accepting an optional `endpoint` parameter in `chat_completion()`. Add cost estimation using approximate rates ($3/1M input, $15/1M output for Claude; $2.50/1M input, $10/1M output for GPT-4o; $0.075/1M for Gemini Flash). Add a GET endpoint `/api/settings/llm-usage` in demo_app.py that returns the accumulated stats with a `reset_at` timestamp. Add a POST `/api/settings/llm-usage/reset` endpoint.

tasks.title: Request/response logging middleware
tasks.description: Add Flask middleware in backend/demo_app.py that logs all API requests in structured JSON format. Use Flask's `@app.before_request` to record the start time, and `@app.after_request` to compute duration and log the entry. Each log entry should include: ISO 8601 timestamp, HTTP method, request path, query string, status code, response time in milliseconds, request content-length, response content-length, and client IP. Use Python's `logging` module with a custom JSON formatter. If the `LOG_FILE` environment variable is set, add a FileHandler that writes to that path in addition to stdout. Exclude /api/health from logging to avoid noise from health check polling. Add a request ID (UUID4) to each request and include it in the response headers as `X-Request-ID` for debugging.

tasks.title: Simulation summary statistics endpoint
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/summary` in backend/demo_app.py that computes and returns aggregate statistics for a simulation. Reuse the existing `_generate_agent_actions()` function to get all actions, then compute: total_posts (count CREATE_POST actions), unique_agents (distinct agent_name values), avg_engagement_score (derive from action density per agent), top_topics (extract from post content using keyword extraction — look for capitalized phrases and known topics from the knowledge graph), sentiment_distribution (simple positive/negative/neutral counts based on keyword matching against word lists), and activity_over_time (bucket actions by round_num into groups of 12 rounds each, returning counts per bucket). Return all stats in a single JSON response. Cache the result in `_simulations[sim_id]` to avoid recomputation on subsequent calls.

tasks.title: Agent sentiment tracking data
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/sentiment` in backend/demo_app.py that returns per-agent sentiment scores across simulation rounds. Define positive word lists (e.g., 'impressive', 'excellent', 'resolved', 'improved', 'saved') and negative word lists (e.g., 'frustrated', 'failed', 'struggled', 'concerned', 'risk') as module-level constants. For each agent in the simulation, iterate through their posts by round and compute a sentiment score (-1.0 to 1.0) based on the ratio of positive to negative keywords found in post content. Return a JSON structure: `{agents: [{agent_name, agent_id, sentiments: [{round, score}]}]}`. Use deterministic seeding (based on agent name hash + round) so the data is consistent across calls. Include an `overall_sentiment` field per agent averaging all their round scores.

tasks.title: Competitive mention tracking endpoint
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/competitive` in backend/demo_app.py. Define a competitors list: ['Zendesk', 'Freshdesk', 'HubSpot', 'Salesforce Service Cloud', 'Help Scout']. Scan all agent post content from `_generate_agent_actions()` for case-insensitive mentions of each competitor. Return: `mention_counts` (object mapping competitor name to total count), `mentions_over_time` (array of {round, competitor, count} for timeline visualization), `top_mentioners` (agents who mention competitors most, with counts), and `mention_sentiment` (for each competitor mention, classify surrounding context as positive/negative/neutral using the same keyword approach from the sentiment endpoint). The existing post content in `action_templates` already contains Zendesk and Freshdesk references, so this endpoint will return real data from the simulation.

tasks.title: Agent influence network endpoint
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/influence` in backend/demo_app.py that returns a directed graph of agent-to-agent influence. Build the influence model using three signals from the simulation data: 1) Reply patterns — if Agent A replies to Agent B's post, A is influenced by B (weight based on frequency). 2) Topic adoption — if Agent B starts posting about a topic that Agent A posted about first, infer influence (weight based on time gap). 3) Sentiment alignment — if Agent B's sentiment shifts toward Agent A's position over time, infer influence. Return a graph structure: `{nodes: [{id, name, influence_score, persona_type}], edges: [{source, target, weight, type}]}` where type is 'reply', 'topic_adoption', or 'sentiment_alignment'. Use the 15 agents defined in `_generate_agent_actions()`. Generate deterministic edges using a seeded random based on sim_id.

tasks.title: Round-by-round heatmap data endpoint
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/heatmap` in backend/demo_app.py that returns a matrix suitable for heatmap visualization. Rows represent the 15 agents (from `_generate_agent_actions()`), columns represent simulation rounds (bucketed into groups of 6 rounds for manageability — yielding ~24 columns for 144 rounds). Cell values represent engagement intensity: count of actions by that agent in that round bucket, normalized to 0.0-1.0 range. Return: `{rows: [agent_name, ...], columns: [round_label, ...], values: [[float, ...], ...], color_scale: {min: 0, max: 1, low_color: '#1e3a5f', high_color: '#ff5600'}}`. Use deterministic random seeding so the heatmap is consistent. Include overall row and column totals as `row_sums` and `col_sums` arrays.

tasks.title: Pydantic-style request validation for POST endpoints
tasks.description: Add request validation to all POST endpoints in backend/demo_app.py using marshmallow (install via pip). Define schema classes for each endpoint: `GraphBuildSchema` (seed_text: required string, min 10 chars), `SimulationCreateSchema` (graph_id: optional string), `SimulationPrepareSchema` (simulation_id: required string), `SimulationStartSchema` (simulation_id: required string), `ChatMessageSchema` (message: required string, max 2000 chars; simulation_id: optional string), `GenerateSeedSchema` (topic: required string; industry: optional string; persona_types: optional list of strings; tone: optional string with choices). Create a `validate_request` decorator that validates `request.get_json()` against the schema and returns 422 with field-level error details (`{errors: {field: [messages]}}`) on validation failure. Apply the decorator to all POST endpoints. Add marshmallow to the Dockerfile pip install.

tasks.title: Streaming chat in ChatView.vue (EventSource/SSE)
tasks.description: Update frontend/src/views/ChatView.vue to support streaming responses from the new `/api/report/chat/stream` SSE endpoint. Replace the current axios POST call with a fetch()-based SSE reader (EventSource does not support POST bodies, so use fetch with ReadableStream). Parse incoming SSE events: on 'start' event, create a new assistant message bubble; on 'data' events containing tokens, append each token to the message content reactively; on 'done', finalize the message. Add a typing cursor animation (blinking `|` character) at the end of the message while streaming. Add a 'Stop generating' button (visible only during streaming) that calls `reader.cancel()` on the ReadableStream. Fall back to the existing non-streaming POST `/api/report/chat` endpoint if the stream connection fails. Update the API layer in frontend/src/api/chat.js to export both `sendMessage()` and `streamMessage()` functions.

tasks.title: Streaming interview in AgentProfileView.vue
tasks.description: Update the interview tab in frontend/src/views/AgentProfileView.vue to support SSE streaming for LLM-powered agent interview responses. The interview currently sends questions via POST to a chat-like endpoint. Add a streaming mode that uses the same fetch-based SSE approach as ChatView.vue: on submit, start a stream request with the agent's persona context in the system prompt, show a typing indicator (animated dots), and append tokens to the response bubble as they arrive. Add a 'Cancel' button visible during streaming. If the stream endpoint is unavailable or fails, fall back gracefully to the existing synchronous POST response. Share the SSE parsing logic with ChatView.vue by extracting it into a composable: `frontend/src/composables/useSSEStream.js`. The composable should export a `startStream(url, body, onToken, onDone, onError)` function.

tasks.title: Generate Seed button in ScenarioBuilderView.vue
tasks.description: Add an AI-powered seed text generation button to frontend/src/views/ScenarioBuilderView.vue next to the seed text textarea. The button should have a sparkle/wand icon (use an inline SVG or unicode character) and label 'Generate with AI'. On click, call the new `/api/scenario/generate-seed` POST endpoint with the currently selected scenario's topic, industry, and persona types from the form state. Show a loading spinner inside the button during the request. When the response arrives, animate the generated text appearing in the textarea character-by-character using a requestAnimationFrame loop (typing effect at ~30 chars/second). Add a 'Regenerate' affordance that clears and regenerates. If the API call fails (no LLM configured), show a toast notification explaining that an LLM API key is needed and fall back to the pre-filled template seed text. Disable the button while text is being animated.

tasks.title: LLM temperature/model selection in SettingsView.vue
tasks.description: Enhance the LLM configuration section in frontend/src/views/SettingsView.vue. Add: a provider dropdown with options for Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini (the new provider); a model selection dropdown that dynamically shows available models for the selected provider (Anthropic: claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-haiku-35; OpenAI: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini; Gemini: gemini-1.5-flash, gemini-1.5-pro); a temperature slider with range 0.0-1.0 in 0.1 increments showing the current value; and a system prompt customization textarea (default text: 'You are a GTM strategy analyst...'). Persist all settings to the Pinia settings store (frontend/src/stores/settings.js) which already saves to localStorage. Add a POST endpoint `/api/settings/llm` in demo_app.py that accepts and applies these settings server-side. Wire the 'Test Connection' button to use the selected provider/model combination.

tasks.title: Token usage display in SettingsView.vue
tasks.description: Add a 'Token Usage' dashboard card to frontend/src/views/SettingsView.vue below the LLM configuration section. Display: total tokens used (large number), breakdown by provider as a simple horizontal stacked bar chart (using inline SVG or a lightweight chart), estimated cost in USD, and a 'since' timestamp showing when tracking started. Poll the `/api/settings/llm-usage` GET endpoint every 30 seconds using a `setInterval` that starts on component mount and clears on unmount (use `onUnmounted` lifecycle hook). Add a 'Reset Usage' button that calls POST `/api/settings/llm-usage/reset` and refreshes the display. If the endpoint returns zero usage, show an empty state message: 'No LLM usage recorded yet. Start a chat or generate a report to see token usage.' Style the card consistently with existing settings sections using brand tokens.

tasks.title: Engagement velocity metrics in SimulationPanel.vue
tasks.description: Add a real-time engagement velocity indicator to the metrics section of frontend/src/components/simulation/SimulationPanel.vue. The component already displays total actions, replies, likes, and reposts. Add a new metric card showing: 'Engagement Velocity' as the label, posts-per-minute as the primary number (calculated from total_actions_count / elapsed simulated time), an up/down arrow icon indicating acceleration or deceleration compared to the previous measurement, and a tiny sparkline SVG (inline, ~100x30px) showing the rolling 5-round average of actions per round. Calculate velocity from the existing polling data available via the `useSimulationPolling` composable (which provides `current_round`, `total_actions_count`, and `simulated_hours`). Update the sparkline data on each poll interval. Use green for acceleration and red for deceleration with Intercom brand colors.

tasks.title: More varied agent action content generation
tasks.description: Enhance the `_generate_agent_actions()` function and `action_templates` in backend/demo_app.py to produce more diverse, realistic post content. Currently there are ~8 Twitter posts, ~3 Reddit posts, and ~5 reply templates. Expand each category to 20+ templates. Add: industry-specific terminology pools (healthcare agents should mention HIPAA, patient experience; fintech agents should mention SOC2, transaction volumes), varying post lengths (some short hot-takes of 1-2 sentences, some detailed analyses of 4-5 sentences), occasional questions ('Has anyone else tried Fin for technical support tickets? Curious about accuracy on complex issues.'), selective emoji usage for certain persona types (Support Managers use more emoji than CTOs), and @mention patterns between agents (e.g., 'Interesting point from @SarahChen about pilot results'). Map specific content pools to specific agent personas based on their role and company to make the simulation feel more authentic.

tasks.title: Decision journey stage tracking in demo_app.py
tasks.description: Add a decision journey tracking system to backend/demo_app.py that models each agent's progression through buyer stages: Awareness, Interest, Consideration, Intent, Evaluation, Decision. Create a `_compute_agent_journeys(sim_id)` function that takes the simulation state and derives each agent's current stage based on their activity signals: agents with only 'view' actions are in Awareness; agents who reply or engage are in Interest; agents discussing specific features or comparisons are in Consideration; agents expressing purchase intent keywords are in Intent; agents in active competitive evaluation are in Evaluation; agents who have posted positive decision signals are in Decision. Store as `{agent_name: {stage, entered_at_round, signals: [...]}}`. Add a GET endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/journeys` returning all agent journeys. Use deterministic progression based on agent index and round number so results are reproducible.

tasks.title: Interactive data tables in ReportView.vue
tasks.description: Add sortable, filterable data tables to the report sections in frontend/src/views/ReportView.vue. Create a new component `frontend/src/components/report/DataTable.vue` that accepts `columns` (array of {key, label, sortable, type}) and `rows` (array of objects) props. Implement: click-to-sort on column headers (ascending/descending/none cycle with arrow indicators), a text filter input above the table that searches across all string columns, and a 'Export CSV' button that generates and downloads a CSV file using Blob and URL.createObjectURL. Pre-populate the table with data matching the report's existing markdown tables: agent name, posts count, avg sentiment, engagement score, decision stage, top topics. Fetch the data from the `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/summary` and `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/journeys` endpoints. Style the table with alternating row colors, hover highlight, and brand-consistent borders.

tasks.title: PDF export endpoint
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/report/{task_id}/export/pdf` in backend/demo_app.py that generates a branded PDF report. Install `weasyprint` via pip (or `reportlab` as fallback if weasyprint has system dependency issues). Construct an HTML template string that includes: a cover page with 'MiroFish GTM Analysis' title and Intercom blue (#2068FF) accent bar, an executive summary section pulling from REPORT_SECTIONS[0], data tables rendered as HTML tables with styled headers, and chart placeholders (since D3 charts are client-side, generate simple matplotlib bar charts for engagement by persona and sentiment distribution, encode as base64 PNG images embedded in the HTML). Apply Intercom brand colors throughout: blue headers, navy text, system-ui font family. Convert the HTML to PDF using weasyprint's `HTML(string=html).write_pdf()`. Return the PDF with `Content-Type: application/pdf` and `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=report.pdf`.

tasks.title: Report chart hover tooltips + click-to-drill
tasks.description: Enhance all D3 chart components in frontend/src/components/report/ReportCharts.vue with interactive tooltips and drill-down capability. Create a shared tooltip div (positioned absolute, hidden by default) styled with dark background, white text, rounded corners, and a subtle shadow. On mouseover of any chart data element (bar, line point, pie slice), show the tooltip near the cursor with: the data label, exact value, and percentage of total. Use D3's `d3.pointer()` for positioning. On click of a data element, emit a 'drill' event with the data key — the parent ReportView.vue should handle this by scrolling to or opening a detail panel showing filtered data for that segment (e.g., clicking a persona bar shows that persona's activity feed). Add smooth fade-in/out transitions on the tooltip (150ms). Ensure tooltips stay within viewport bounds by checking position against window dimensions.

tasks.title: Additional chart types (scatter, horizontal bar)
tasks.description: Create two new D3 visualization components in frontend/src/components/report/. First, `ScatterPlot.vue`: accepts `data` prop as array of {x, y, label, color, size}, renders an SVG scatter plot with axes, axis labels, and sized/colored circles. Plot engagement score (x-axis) vs sentiment (y-axis) with each agent as a dot, colored by persona type. Add zoom behavior with d3-zoom. Second, `HorizontalBarChart.vue`: accepts `data` prop as array of {label, value, color}, renders horizontal bars sorted by value descending — useful for ranked metrics like 'Top Agents by Engagement'. Include value labels at bar ends and subtle gridlines. Register both components in ReportCharts.vue alongside existing charts. Style with Intercom brand tokens: #2068FF for primary bars, #ff5600 for accent, #050505 for text, system-ui fonts.

tasks.title: Agent sentiment timeline visualization
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/SentimentTimeline.vue` — a multi-line D3 chart showing per-agent sentiment scores over simulation rounds. Fetch data from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/sentiment`. Render one line per agent, color-coded by persona type (VP Support = #2068FF blue, CX Director = #ff5600 orange, IT Leader = #A0F purple, Operations = #090 green, Finance = #FFB800 amber). Add: a legend showing agent names with color swatches (clickable to toggle line visibility), d3-zoom for horizontal zoom/pan to inspect specific round ranges, and a hover tooltip that snaps to the nearest data point showing agent name, round number, and exact sentiment score. Add a smoothing toggle that switches between raw values and a 5-round moving average. The chart should be responsive — recalculate dimensions on window resize using a ResizeObserver.

tasks.title: Competitive mention tracking visualization
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/CompetitiveMentions.vue` that visualizes data from the `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/competitive` endpoint. Include three visualization sections: 1) A stacked bar chart showing competitor mentions over time (x-axis = round buckets, y-axis = mention count, stacked by competitor — each competitor gets a distinct color). 2) A donut/pie chart showing overall mention share per competitor with percentage labels. 3) A scrollable data table listing the top competitive quotes with columns: agent name, competitor mentioned, quote excerpt, sentiment badge (positive/negative/neutral). Use D3 for the charts. Add a filter dropdown to select a specific competitor or 'All'. Animate chart transitions when filter changes using D3's transition(). Style competitor colors distinctly: Zendesk=#03363D, Freshdesk=#2C9C6F, HubSpot=#FF7A59, Salesforce=#00A1E0, Help Scout=#1292EE.

tasks.title: Agent avatar generation from persona
tasks.description: Create a deterministic SVG avatar generator for agents in `frontend/src/utils/avatarGenerator.js`. The function `generateAvatar(name, role, company)` should return an SVG string. Use a hash of the agent's full name as the seed for deterministic randomness. Support three avatar styles selected by hash modulo: 1) Geometric — colored polygon shapes (triangle, hexagon, circle) on a contrasting background. 2) Initials — two-letter initials on a gradient circle background, using colors derived from persona type (VP=blue gradient, Director=orange gradient, Manager=purple gradient, CTO=green gradient). 3) Abstract — layered semi-transparent circles in persona-themed colors. Each avatar should be unique but reproducible for the same agent name. Create a Vue component `frontend/src/components/common/AgentAvatar.vue` that wraps this utility with props for name, role, size (sm/md/lg mapping to 32/48/64px). Replace the current `initial` computed property usage in AgentProfileView.vue with this component.

tasks.title: Suggested interview questions per persona
tasks.description: Add a 'Suggested Questions' collapsible panel to the interview tab in frontend/src/views/AgentProfileView.vue. Display 5 contextual interview questions based on the agent's persona attributes. Build a question template system in a new file `frontend/src/utils/interviewQuestions.js` that maps persona roles to relevant questions: VP of Support gets questions about team size, tool evaluation criteria, and budget cycles; CX Directors get questions about customer satisfaction metrics and AI adoption; IT Leaders get integration and security questions; Operations leads get efficiency and cost questions; Finance roles get ROI and procurement timeline questions. Additionally, if the agent has simulation activity data, generate 1-2 questions referencing their specific posts (e.g., 'You mentioned a 47% resolution rate in your Fin pilot — can you elaborate?'). Render as a vertical list of clickable question chips. On click, auto-fill the interview chat input with the selected question and optionally auto-submit.

tasks.title: Batch interview mode (ask all agents)
tasks.description: Add a 'Ask All Agents' button to the interview tab in frontend/src/views/AgentProfileView.vue (or create a dedicated batch interview modal). When clicked, show a text input for the question and a 'Send to All' button. On submit, send the question to the chat/interview endpoint once per agent in the simulation (use the 15 agents from the simulation data), passing each agent's persona context. Display responses in a responsive card grid layout (3 columns on desktop, 1 on mobile) — each card shows: the agent avatar (using the new AgentAvatar component), agent name and role, and their response text. Add a 'Sort by' dropdown: alphabetical, response length, sentiment (most positive first). Add a 'Summary' toggle at the top that uses the LLM to generate a one-paragraph synthesis of all responses. Include a loading state showing skeleton cards while responses stream in. Add the endpoint `/api/interview/batch` to demo_app.py that accepts a question and returns mock responses for all 15 agents.

tasks.title: Agent comparison view (2-3 side by side)
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/views/AgentComparisonView.vue` (or a modal component accessible from the simulation workspace). Add a multi-select agent picker at the top allowing selection of 2-3 agents from the simulation. Once selected, render a side-by-side comparison layout with equal-width columns. For each agent column, show: avatar and name header, a metrics card (posts count, avg sentiment score, engagement score, decision stage from the journeys endpoint), a mini sentiment timeline sparkline, top 3 topics they engaged with, and 2-3 key quotes from their posts. At the bottom, add a 'Comparison Summary' section highlighting key differences: who is more engaged, whose sentiment is more positive, who is further along the decision journey. Add a route `/workspace/:taskId/compare` in the router and a 'Compare Agents' button in SimulationPanel.vue's agent list that opens this view with pre-selected agents.

tasks.title: Interview chat export as markdown
Animated engagement timeline replay
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/simulation/TimelineReplay.vue` — a visual replay component that animates the simulation timeline. Add a scrubber/progress bar at the bottom showing round progression (0 to 144). As the scrubber advances, animate: agent dots appearing on a 2D canvas when they first post, lines connecting agents who reply to each other, pulse animations on agents when they take actions, and a running counter of total actions. Add playback controls: play/pause button, speed selector (1x, 2x, 5x, 10x), and a draggable scrubber for manual seeking. Use requestAnimationFrame for smooth animation. Highlight 'key moments' with callout badges: first competitive mention, highest engagement round, first Decision-stage agent. Fetch timeline data from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/timeline` and action data from the detail endpoint. Add a 'Replay' button to SimulationPanel.vue that opens this component in a full-width overlay.

tasks.title: Word cloud from agent posts
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/WordCloud.vue` using the d3-cloud library (install via pnpm). Fetch all agent post content from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/actions` endpoint. Extract individual words, filter out common stop words (the, is, a, an, and, or, but, in, on, at, to, for, of, with, etc.), and count frequencies. Size each word proportionally to its frequency using d3-cloud's `fontSize` scale. Color words by sentiment: analyze each word's context across posts — words appearing predominantly in positive contexts get green (#090), negative contexts get red (#dc2626), neutral gets gray (#6b7280). Add click interaction: clicking a word filters the parent view to show only posts containing that word (emit an event with the word). Add a 'Regenerate Layout' button since d3-cloud produces different layouts on each render. The component should accept `width` and `height` props with responsive defaults.

tasks.title: Agent influence network force graph
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/InfluenceGraph.vue` using D3's force-directed layout (d3-force). Fetch data from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/influence`. Render an SVG with: nodes as circles sized by `influence_score` (range 20-60px radius), colored by persona type using the same color scheme as SentimentTimeline (VP=#2068FF, CX=#ff5600, IT=#A0F, Ops=#090, Finance=#FFB800). Edges are lines with arrowheads (using SVG marker-end) — thickness proportional to weight, styled with dashed lines for 'sentiment_alignment' type and solid for 'reply' and 'topic_adoption'. Add d3-force simulation with forceCenter, forceCollide, forceManyBody (repulsion), and forceLink. Make nodes draggable with d3-drag. Add zoom/pan with d3-zoom. On node hover, highlight that node's direct connections and dim all others. On node click, show a detail panel with the agent's name, influence score, and list of incoming/outgoing influence connections.

tasks.title: Engagement heatmap visualization
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/EngagementHeatmap.vue` using D3. Fetch data from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/heatmap`. Render a grid where rows represent agents (y-axis) and columns represent round buckets (x-axis). Each cell is a colored rectangle using a sequential color scale: low engagement = cool blue (#1e3a5f), high engagement = warm orange (#ff5600), matching the `color_scale` metadata from the API. Add row labels (agent names, truncated to 15 chars) on the left and column labels (round ranges) on the bottom, rotated 45 degrees for readability. Add hover interaction: on cell hover, show a tooltip with agent name, round range, and exact engagement value. Add marginal summary bars: a bar chart above showing column totals (overall activity per round) and a bar chart on the right showing row totals (overall activity per agent). The component should handle responsive width by adjusting cell size.

tasks.title: Decision journey funnel visualization
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/DecisionFunnel.vue` showing aggregate agent progression through decision stages as a funnel chart. Fetch data from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/journeys`. Count agents at each stage (Awareness, Interest, Consideration, Intent, Evaluation, Decision) and render as a vertical funnel: widest at top (Awareness), narrowing toward bottom (Decision). Each funnel segment is a trapezoid SVG path filled with a gradient from light to dark brand blue. Show: count and percentage labels inside each segment, drop-off rate between stages on the left side (e.g., '33% drop-off'), and average time-to-advance on the right side. Animate the funnel segments on initial render — start collapsed and expand to final widths over 800ms using D3 transitions. On segment click, show a list of agents currently at that stage. Add a 'Compare to Benchmark' toggle that overlays an industry-average funnel outline.

tasks.title: Sparkline mini-charts in metric cards
tasks.description: Create a reusable `frontend/src/components/common/Sparkline.vue` component for embedding tiny trend charts in metric cards. Accept props: `data` (array of numbers), `width` (default 80), `height` (default 24), `color` (default '#2068FF'), `showDot` (boolean, shows dot on last point). Render using an inline SVG with a single `<polyline>` for the line and an optional `<circle>` for the last data point. Scale the data to fit the width/height using simple min/max normalization. Add subtle area fill below the line using a `<polygon>` with 10% opacity of the line color. Add this sparkline to existing metric cards in SimulationPanel.vue: the total actions card shows a sparkline of actions-per-round, the engagement velocity card shows the velocity trend. Also add sparklines to the report summary metrics in ReportView.vue. The component should be purely presentational with no data fetching — parent components pass pre-computed data arrays.

tasks.title: Chart export buttons (PNG/SVG)
tasks.description: Create a reusable `frontend/src/components/common/ChartExportButtons.vue` component that adds PNG and SVG export buttons to any chart container. Accept a `targetRef` prop (template ref pointing to the chart's SVG or container element). For SVG export: serialize the SVG DOM element using `new XMLSerializer().serializeToString()`, add xmlns attribute if missing, create a Blob with type 'image/svg+xml', and trigger download. For PNG export: create an off-screen canvas, draw the SVG onto it using an Image with a data URI (from the serialized SVG as a base64-encoded source), then use `canvas.toBlob()` to create the PNG download. Generate filenames with format `{chartType}-{timestamp}.{ext}`. Add this component to all D3 chart components: ReportCharts.vue, SentimentTimeline.vue, CompetitiveMentions.vue, InfluenceGraph.vue, EngagementHeatmap.vue, DecisionFunnel.vue, WordCloud.vue, ScatterPlot.vue, and HorizontalBarChart.vue. Position the buttons as a small icon group in the top-right corner of each chart.

tasks.title: Ctrl+K command palette
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/ui/CommandPalette.vue` — a modal search overlay triggered by Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on macOS). Register the keyboard shortcut in the App.vue root component using a global keydown listener that checks `(e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k'`. The palette renders as a centered overlay with a search input at top and a filtered results list below. Define command items: page navigation (Home, Scenarios, Settings, Simulations), recent simulations (from simulation store), quick actions ('New Simulation', 'Export Report', 'Open Chat'), and settings shortcuts ('LLM Config', 'Theme Toggle'). Implement fuzzy search using simple substring matching on command titles. Support keyboard navigation: ArrowUp/ArrowDown to move selection highlight, Enter to execute selected command, Escape to close. Each command has an icon, title, subtitle, and action callback. Add a subtle backdrop blur effect. Close on outside click.

tasks.title: Breadcrumb navigation
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/layout/BreadcrumbNav.vue` component that shows the navigation path on every page. Auto-generate breadcrumb items from the current Vue Router route: parse the matched route hierarchy to build segments. Map route names to readable labels: 'landing' -> 'Home', 'scenario-builder' -> 'Scenario Builder', 'workspace' -> 'Simulation Workspace', 'report' -> 'Report', 'chat' -> 'Chat', 'settings' -> 'Settings', 'agent-profile' -> 'Agent Profile'. Each segment (except the last) is a clickable router-link. Separate segments with a '/' or chevron icon. Style with: small text (text-sm), muted color for inactive segments (text-gray-400), bold for current page. Add the component to AppLayout.vue above the main content slot. On the landing page, don't show breadcrumbs (only one level). Include the simulation/scenario name in breadcrumbs when available from the Pinia store.

tasks.title: Notification center
tasks.description: Expand the existing toast system in frontend/src/composables/useToast.js and frontend/src/components/ui/ToastContainer.vue into a full notification center. Add a notification bell icon to AppNav.vue that shows an unread count badge. On click, open a dropdown/drawer showing recent notifications in a scrollable list. Each notification has: type (success/error/warning/info with colored left border), title, message, timestamp (relative — '2 min ago'), and dismiss button. Notifications are triggered for: simulation phase completions, report generation done, export completed, settings saved, API errors, and LLM provider connection status changes. Create a Pinia store `frontend/src/stores/notifications.js` to persist notifications (keep last 50). Toasts still appear as transient overlays, but they also get logged to the notification center for history. Add a 'Clear All' button and 'Mark All Read' button.

tasks.title: Skeleton loading states
tasks.description: Create skeleton loading components that match the layout of actual content. Build three components in frontend/src/components/ui/: `SkeletonCard.vue` (rounded rectangle with shimmer animation matching Card.vue dimensions — title bar, 2-3 text lines, optional footer), `SkeletonChart.vue` (rectangle with placeholder axis lines and random-height bar shapes, shimmer overlay), and `SkeletonTable.vue` (header row + N body rows of rectangular placeholders with alternating opacity). The shimmer animation should use CSS: a gradient (`linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, rgba(255,255,255,0.3), transparent)`) animated with `@keyframes` sliding left-to-right over 1.5s infinite. Apply these skeletons: ReportView.vue shows SkeletonChart while report generates, SimulationPanel.vue shows SkeletonCard for metric cards while first data loads, the landing page shows SkeletonCard for scenario cards while fetching, and data tables show SkeletonTable while loading. Replace existing loading spinners where skeleton screens provide better perceived performance.

tasks.title: ARIA labels + keyboard accessibility
tasks.description: Audit all interactive components in the frontend and add comprehensive ARIA attributes. In `frontend/src/components/common/`: add `role='button'` and `aria-label` to Button.vue, `role='dialog'` and `aria-modal='true'` to Modal.vue, `aria-live='polite'` to StatusIndicator.vue. In views: add `aria-label` to all icon-only buttons (close, export, settings gear), `aria-expanded` to collapsible sections, `aria-selected` to tab interfaces (ChatView tabs, AgentProfileView tabs, WorkspacePhaseNav). Add a 'Skip to main content' link as the first focusable element in AppLayout.vue that jumps to `#main-content`. Ensure all form inputs in SettingsView.vue and ScenarioBuilderView.vue have associated `<label>` elements or `aria-label`. Add `aria-live='assertive'` regions for toast notifications and `aria-live='polite'` for simulation status updates. Test tab order on every page to ensure logical flow.

tasks.title: Focus management for modals
tasks.description: Implement proper focus trapping for all modal/dialog components. Create a composable `frontend/src/composables/useFocusTrap.js` that: on activation, saves the previously focused element (`document.activeElement`), finds all focusable elements within the trap container (inputs, buttons, links, elements with tabindex), moves focus to the first focusable element, and adds a keydown listener that wraps Tab/Shift+Tab focus within the container boundaries. On deactivation, restore focus to the saved element. Apply this composable to: Modal.vue (frontend/src/components/common/Modal.vue), ConfirmDialog.vue (frontend/src/components/ui/ConfirmDialog.vue), the new CommandPalette.vue, and any future overlay components. Also handle the Escape key to close modals. Add `overflow: hidden` to document.body when a modal is open to prevent background scrolling. Ensure focus trap activates on `nextTick` after the modal's enter transition completes.

tasks.title: High contrast mode
tasks.description: Add a high contrast mode toggle to SettingsView.vue alongside the existing dark mode toggle. When activated, apply a 'high-contrast' class to the `<html>` element. Define CSS custom property overrides in `frontend/src/assets/brand-tokens.css` under `.high-contrast`: increase all text contrast ratios to meet WCAG AAA (7:1 minimum) — darken light text colors, lighten dark backgrounds; thicken all borders from 1px to 2px; add text-decoration underline to all `<a>` links; increase focus ring width from 2px to 4px with a high-contrast color (black on light, white on dark); make button borders always visible (not just on focus/hover). Persist the preference in localStorage via the Pinia settings store. Respect the `prefers-contrast: more` media query by auto-enabling on first visit for users with system high-contrast preferences. Ensure D3 chart colors are distinguishable — add pattern fills as alternative to color-only differentiation.

tasks.title: Print-optimized report styles
tasks.description: Add `@media print` CSS rules specifically for frontend/src/views/ReportView.vue. Create a scoped style block or a dedicated `frontend/src/assets/print.css` imported in the report view. Print rules should: hide AppNav, AppFooter, sidebar navigation, all interactive controls (buttons, dropdowns, inputs), the chat widget, and any fixed/sticky elements. Ensure D3 charts render properly at print resolution by setting explicit width/height on SVG elements (remove responsive CSS that might collapse them). Add CSS `page-break-before: always` to each report section heading (h2). Add a print header with report title and generation date using `@page { @top-center { content: 'MiroFish GTM Report' } }`. Set `@page { size: A4; margin: 2cm; }`. Force all colors to print-friendly values — ensure dark backgrounds invert to white with dark text. Add a 'Print Report' button in the ReportView.vue toolbar that calls `window.print()`.

tasks.title: Guided tour walkthrough
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/demo/GuidedTour.vue` that implements a multi-step spotlight tour of the application. The tour highlights UI elements in sequence by: darkening the rest of the page with a semi-transparent overlay (rgba(0,0,0,0.7)), cutting out a spotlight rectangle around the target element using CSS clip-path or SVG mask, and showing an explanatory tooltip positioned near the highlighted element. Define tour steps as an array: 1) Landing page hero — 'Welcome to MiroFish', 2) Scenario cards — 'Choose a GTM scenario', 3) Scenario builder seed input — 'Customize your simulation', 4) Graph visualization — 'Explore the knowledge graph', 5) Simulation panel — 'Watch agents interact in real-time', 6) Report view — 'Read AI-generated insights', 7) Chat interface — 'Ask follow-up questions'. Each step has a target CSS selector, tooltip text, and position (top/bottom/left/right). Add Previous/Next/Skip buttons. Track completion in localStorage. Trigger on first visit to the landing page.

tasks.title: Preset demo data with story arc
tasks.description: Create a complete preset simulation dataset in `backend/demo_data/preset_simulation.json` that tells a compelling, pre-scripted story. Design 15 agents across 3 persona clusters: 5 'Early Adopters' (positive sentiment, quick to Decision stage), 5 'Cautious Evaluators' (initially skeptical, gradually warming), and 5 'Competitive Defenders' (loyal to current vendor, resistant). Script 20 rounds with specific narrative beats: Rounds 1-5: Awareness phase, initial reactions. Rounds 6-10: Interest builds, first competitive comparisons. Round 12: Competitive disruption event — a simulated industry report drops favoring Intercom, triggering a wave of engagement. Rounds 13-17: Rapid progression through Consideration/Intent. Rounds 18-20: Resolution — Early Adopters reach Decision, Evaluators reach Intent, Defenders remain in Consideration. Add a 'Demo Mode' button to LandingView.vue that loads this dataset, bypassing the real simulation flow, and navigates directly to a pre-populated report.

tasks.title: Replay mode (accelerated playback)
tasks.description: Add replay functionality to completed simulations. When a simulation is complete (status = 'completed'), show a 'Replay' button in SimulationPanel.vue. On click, reset the displayed state to round 0 and replay the simulation at accelerated speed. Add speed controls: 1x (real-time replay of the accelerated simulation), 2x, 5x, 10x — each multiplier reduces the delay between displayed rounds. Use `setInterval` with configurable delay: base delay is 500ms per round at 1x, so 10x plays at 50ms per round. During replay, update all metric cards, the activity feed, and the timeline chart progressively as if the simulation were running live. Show a replay progress bar at the top of SimulationPanel.vue with the scrubber position. Add play/pause toggle and a 'Jump to End' button. Store the complete simulation data (all rounds) in memory during first playback so scrubbing backward is instant.

tasks.title: Screenshot-ready view mode
tasks.description: Add a 'Presentation Mode' toggle accessible from the PresenterToolbar.vue component (frontend/src/components/demo/PresenterToolbar.vue) or via Ctrl+Shift+P keyboard shortcut. When activated: hide AppNav and AppFooter, hide all interactive controls (buttons, inputs, dropdowns) that aren't essential for viewing, maximize the content area to fill the viewport, increase base font size by 20% using a CSS class on `<html>`, add a subtle 'MiroFish by Intercom' watermark in the bottom-right corner (low opacity, non-intrusive), and disable hover tooltips (set a global `presentationMode` flag in Pinia that components check before showing tooltips). The mode should be optimized for screen sharing and screenshot capture. Add a floating 'Exit Presentation Mode' button in the bottom-right that appears on mouse movement and fades after 3 seconds of inactivity. Persist mode in sessionStorage (not localStorage — only for current session).

tasks.title: AI narration mode during simulation
tasks.description: Add an 'AI Narration' toggle to SimulationPanel.vue that, when enabled, shows a text overlay describing what is happening in the simulation in natural language. On each round update (from the polling data), generate a narration sentence. If LLM is configured, call a new endpoint `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/narrate` with the current round's actions and get an LLM-generated description (e.g., 'Round 12: Sarah Chen just shifted from Consideration to Intent after engaging with 3 positive Fin deployment posts. Meanwhile, Nathan Lee remains skeptical, posting about vendor lock-in concerns.'). If no LLM is available, use template-based narration: pick the most interesting action from the round (a Decision stage change > a competitive mention > a new post) and fill in a template. Display the narration in a semi-transparent overlay bar at the bottom of the simulation panel. Animate text entry with a typewriter effect. Keep a scrollable history of past narrations accessible via a 'History' toggle.

tasks.title: One-click quick-start demo
tasks.description: Add a prominent 'Quick Demo' button to LandingView.vue, positioned above or alongside the scenario cards. Style it as a large, eye-catching CTA with the Intercom blue (#2068FF) background, white text, and a play icon. On click, the button should: 1) Show a brief loading state with 'Preparing demo...', 2) Call `/api/graph/build` with the outbound_campaign scenario's seed text, 3) Immediately navigate to the simulation workspace, 4) Auto-advance through phases: wait for graph build (the existing 6-second timer), then auto-trigger simulation start, 5) Play the simulation at 3x speed (using the DEMO_SPEED mechanism), 6) On completion, auto-navigate to the report view. The entire flow should require zero configuration — no scenario selection, no parameter tuning. Add a backend endpoint `/api/demo/quick-start` that orchestrates this by creating the graph task, simulation, and report in sequence, returning all task IDs. If an LLM key is configured, enable chat on the report; otherwise, use fallback mode.

tasks.title: Settings validation with inline feedback
tasks.description: Add real-time validation to all settings inputs in frontend/src/views/SettingsView.vue. For each input field, add a validation function that runs on input/blur events: API key fields should match expected patterns (Anthropic keys start with 'sk-ant-', OpenAI with 'sk-', Gemini with 'AI'), URL fields should match a URL regex, numeric fields (temperature, agent count) should be within their defined ranges. Display validation state visually: valid inputs get a green checkmark icon (position: absolute, right side of input), invalid inputs get a red outline (border-red-500) and an error message below in small red text. Create a reusable `ValidatedInput.vue` wrapper component that accepts validation rules as props (array of {validator: Function, message: string}). The 'Save Settings' button should be disabled when any validation error exists. Show a summary count of errors above the save button if any exist.

tasks.title: Import/export settings as JSON
tasks.description: Add 'Export Settings' and 'Import Settings' buttons to the bottom of frontend/src/views/SettingsView.vue. Export: read all current settings from the Pinia settings store, add a `version` field (1) and `exported_at` ISO timestamp, serialize to JSON with 2-space indentation, create a Blob, and trigger download as `mirofish-settings-{date}.json`. Import: open a file picker (accept .json only), read the file, parse JSON, validate the schema (check for required keys: version, at minimum), show a diff preview modal comparing current settings vs. imported settings (use a simple side-by-side layout highlighting changed values in yellow), and on 'Apply' confirmation, update the Pinia store. Handle version migration: if imported settings have version < current, transform the data structure (future-proofing). Show a toast notification on successful import/export. Handle errors gracefully: malformed JSON shows a clear error message, incompatible versions show a warning.

tasks.title: Simulation presets (quick configs)
tasks.description: Add simulation preset cards to frontend/src/views/ScenarioBuilderView.vue above the detailed configuration form. Define 4 presets as a constant array: 'Quick Demo' (5 agents, 10 rounds, ~30 seconds — 'Fast overview for stakeholder demos'), 'Full Simulation' (15 agents, 144 rounds, ~35 seconds — 'Complete simulation with all agent types'), 'Deep Analysis' (20 agents, 200 rounds, ~60 seconds — 'Extended simulation for detailed behavioral analysis'), and 'Custom' (user-defined values — 'Configure your own parameters'). Render as a row of 4 cards (2x2 on mobile), each with: preset name, agent/round counts, estimated duration, one-line description, and a 'Select' button. Selecting a preset auto-fills the form fields below (agent count, duration, platform mode). The 'Custom' card highlights the form for manual editing. Add a subtle check icon on the selected preset card. Store the selected preset in the form state so it can be referenced in the simulation metadata.

tasks.title: Zep connection test with graph stats
tasks.description: Enhance the Zep settings section in frontend/src/views/SettingsView.vue. The existing 'Test Connection' button should call a new backend endpoint `/api/settings/zep/test` (add to demo_app.py). This endpoint should: check if ZEP_API_KEY is set, attempt to call the Zep Cloud API (GET request to the Zep API health endpoint or graph stats endpoint), and return connection status plus graph statistics if available: node count, edge count, and last_updated timestamp. In the frontend, show the test result in a status card below the API key input: green success card with 'Connected' badge and stats (node count, edge count, last sync time), or red error card with specific error message (invalid key, network error, timeout). Add a loading spinner on the button during the test. If Zep is not configured (no API key), show an info message: 'Zep Cloud provides persistent memory for agent conversations. Add your API key to enable.' with a link to zep.ai.

tasks.title: Custom persona type creation
tasks.description: Add a 'Custom Personas' section to frontend/src/views/SettingsView.vue (or as a sub-section of the ScenarioBuilderView.vue). Include a 'Create Persona' button that opens a modal form with fields: name (text, e.g., 'Product Manager'), role description (textarea, e.g., 'Mid-level PM evaluating customer support tools for product-led growth'), typical behavior patterns (textarea, comma-separated — 'data-driven, asks for case studies, focuses on integration APIs'), engagement tendencies (dropdown: 'High Engagement', 'Moderate', 'Low/Observer'), and vocabulary style (dropdown: 'Technical', 'Business', 'Casual', 'Executive'). On save, POST to a new endpoint `/api/settings/personas` in demo_app.py that stores custom personas in memory (module-level dict). The saved personas appear in the ScenarioBuilderView persona type multiselect alongside the 4 defaults. Show saved custom personas as editable cards with Edit and Delete buttons. Persist to backend; on server restart, defaults restore.

tasks.title: Executive summary one-pager generation
tasks.description: Add a GET endpoint `/api/report/{task_id}/executive-summary` in backend/demo_app.py that generates a concise executive summary. If LLM is configured, construct a prompt that includes the report's key metrics (from REPORT_SECTIONS), simulation summary stats, and top agent insights, then ask the LLM to produce a structured summary with: 'Key Finding' (one sentence), 'Top 3 Insights' (bullet points), 'Recommended Actions' (numbered list), and 'Risk Factors' (bullet points). If no LLM, return a pre-written template executive summary based on the outbound_campaign scenario data. In the frontend, add an 'Executive Summary' tab or button to ReportView.vue that fetches and displays this content in a single-page styled layout: white card with Intercom blue top border, large heading, structured sections with icons, and a 'Share' button. The one-pager should be suitable for screenshot-sharing with executives — no scrolling needed on a 1080p screen.

tasks.title: Report comparison view (side by side)
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/views/ReportComparisonView.vue` accessible via route `/reports/compare`. Add a 'Compare Reports' button to the SimulationsView.vue page that allows selecting two completed simulations. The comparison view renders two report summaries side by side in a split-column layout. Align matching sections (Executive Summary next to Executive Summary, Engagement next to Engagement). For each metric that appears in both reports, calculate and display the delta: absolute difference and percentage change. Color-code deltas: green (#090) for improvements (higher engagement, more agents reaching Decision stage), red (#dc2626) for declines. Show a summary comparison card at the top: 'Report A vs Report B — 3 metrics improved, 1 declined, 2 unchanged'. If only one simulation exists, show a message suggesting running another simulation first. Add the route to frontend/src/router/index.js and a navigation link in the breadcrumb.

tasks.title: Confidence intervals on metrics
tasks.description: Add confidence interval calculation to key metrics in the report backend. Create a utility function `_bootstrap_ci(data, n_samples=1000, confidence=0.95)` in demo_app.py that computes confidence intervals using bootstrap resampling: repeatedly sample from the data with replacement, compute the statistic, then take the 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles for the 95% CI. Apply this to: engagement scores per persona type, sentiment averages, and conversion rates. Modify the `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/summary` response to include a `confidence_intervals` object alongside each metric, formatted as `{lower: float, upper: float, confidence: 0.95}`. In the frontend ReportView.vue, display confidence intervals as '±range' next to metric values (e.g., '34.7% ±2.3%'). On D3 charts, add shaded CI bands around data lines using SVG `<path>` elements with low-opacity fills. Since this is demo data with deterministic seeding, the CIs will be illustrative but structurally correct.

tasks.title: Report annotations / notes
tasks.description: Add an annotation system to ReportView.vue that allows clicking any chart or metric to add a text note. Create an `AnnotationMarker.vue` component that renders as a numbered circle (1, 2, 3...) positioned at the click point. On click of any chart area or metric card, show a popover with a textarea for entering a note and 'Save'/'Cancel' buttons. Store annotations in a reactive array in the report view: `{id, target_type, target_id, position: {x, y}, text, created_at}`. Display saved annotations as numbered markers on the report — hover to show the note in a popover. Support edit (click marker to reopen editor) and delete (trash icon in popover). Add a 'Notes Panel' toggle in the report toolbar that shows all annotations in a sidebar list for easy navigation. Persist annotations to a new POST/GET endpoint `/api/report/{task_id}/annotations` in demo_app.py (stored in memory). Annotations survive page navigation but not server restart.

tasks.title: Shareable report URL
tasks.description: Add shareable report URL generation to ReportView.vue. When a user clicks 'Share Report' (add this button to the report toolbar), generate a unique share token by calling a new POST endpoint `/api/report/{task_id}/share` in demo_app.py. The backend generates a UUID token, stores the mapping `{token: task_id}` in a module-level dict `_shared_reports`, and returns the full URL: `{base_url}/shared/{token}`. Add a route `/shared/:token` in the Vue router that renders a read-only version of ReportView.vue (hide edit controls, annotation creation, and chat button). The shared route fetches report data via a new GET endpoint `/api/report/shared/{token}` that looks up the task_id from the token. In the frontend, show a 'Copy Link' button that uses `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` with a toast confirmation 'Link copied!'. Shared reports expire after 24 hours (check timestamp on access). Show an 'Expired' page if the token is no longer valid.

tasks.title: Sankey diagram for agent journeys
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/SankeyDiagram.vue` showing agent flow through decision stages. Install d3-sankey via pnpm. Fetch journey data from `/api/simulation/{sim_id}/journeys`. Build the Sankey data structure: nodes represent each decision stage at two time points (initial state on the left, final state on the right — e.g., 'Awareness (Start)', 'Interest (Start)', ... , 'Decision (End)'). Links connect each agent's starting stage to their ending stage, with link value = number of agents making that specific transition. Color links by outcome: agents reaching Decision or Evaluation are green (#090), agents staying in Awareness or Interest are gray (#9ca3af), and agents who regressed are red (#dc2626). Add hover interaction: highlight the specific flow path on link hover, showing count and agent names in a tooltip. Label each node with the stage name and agent count. The diagram should clearly show where agents 'drop off' or 'accelerate' through the funnel.

tasks.title: Treemap for topic distribution
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/TopicTreemap.vue` using D3's treemap layout (d3-hierarchy). Build the data hierarchy from simulation content: outer level = topic categories (derived from the knowledge graph topic groupings in demo_app.py — e.g., 'AI & Automation', 'Competitive', 'Cost & ROI', 'Compliance'), inner level = specific topics (e.g., under 'AI & Automation': 'Fin Agent Deployment', 'AI Accuracy', 'Agent Augmentation'). Rectangle size = total mention count across all agent posts. Rectangle color = average sentiment of posts mentioning that topic (green for positive, red for negative, using a diverging color scale). Add zoom interaction: click an outer category to zoom into its children (animate the treemap transition smoothly over 500ms), click background or a back button to zoom out. Add hover tooltips showing: topic name, mention count, sentiment score, and top agents discussing this topic. Use `d3.treemap().padding(2).round(true)` for clean rectangle rendering with visible gaps between cells.

tasks.title: Geographic engagement heatmap
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/report/GeoHeatmap.vue` displaying simulated geographic distribution of agent engagement. Since the simulation agents have company attributes (Acme SaaS, MedFirst Health, etc.), assign simulated geographic locations to each company in a constant map within the component: spread across US regions (West Coast tech, East Coast finance, Midwest healthcare, Southeast retail, etc.). Use a simple US map SVG (inline or from a lightweight topojson file) with state/region boundaries. Color each region based on aggregate engagement intensity of agents in that region, using a sequential color scale (light blue to dark Intercom blue #2068FF). Add hover interaction: on region hover, show a tooltip with region name, number of agents, total engagement score, and top agent names. Add a legend showing the color scale with min/max values. Include a toggle to switch between 'Engagement' and 'Sentiment' color modes. This is illustrative data for the demo — clearly label it as 'Simulated Geographic Distribution'.

tasks.title: E2E test for full simulation flow
tasks.description: Write an end-to-end test using Playwright (install @playwright/test via pnpm in frontend/) covering the complete user journey. Create `frontend/e2e/simulation-flow.spec.js`. The test should: 1) Navigate to the landing page and verify scenario cards render, 2) Click the first scenario card, verify navigation to ScenarioBuilderView, 3) Verify seed text is pre-filled, click 'Run Simulation', 4) Wait for graph build phase to complete (poll for progress bar to reach 100%), 5) Verify SimulationPanel renders with metrics updating, 6) Wait for simulation completion (may need to increase DEMO_SPEED via env var for test speed), 7) Click 'Generate Report', verify ReportView renders with chapters, 8) Navigate to Chat, send a test message, verify a response appears, 9) Verify navigation back works. Configure playwright.config.js with: baseURL pointing to the dev server, webServer config to start both backend and frontend, and a timeout of 60 seconds. Test both with and without LLM keys (use the fallback path).

tasks.title: API integration tests for demo_app.py
tasks.description: Write pytest integration tests in `backend/tests/test_demo_app.py` for all demo_app.py endpoints. Use Flask's test client (`app.test_client()`). Test cases should cover: GET /api/health returns 200 with status 'ok', GET /api/gtm/scenarios returns the 4 scenario templates, GET /api/gtm/scenarios/{id} returns correct scenario and 404 for invalid ID, POST /api/graph/build returns a task_id, GET /api/graph/task/{id} returns progress that increases over time (test with mocked time.time), GET /api/graph/data/{id} returns nodes and edges with correct counts, POST /api/simulation/create returns a sim_id, GET /api/simulation/{id}/run-status returns increasing progress, GET /api/simulation/{id}/timeline returns round data, GET /api/report/generate returns chapters, POST /api/report/chat returns a response. Mock LLM calls using `unittest.mock.patch` on `llm_client.chat_completion`. Add fixtures for creating pre-built simulation state. Add a conftest.py with the test client fixture. Target >90% endpoint coverage.

tasks.title: Unit tests for visualization components
tasks.description: Write Vitest unit tests for all new Vue visualization components created in groups 4-6. Create test files alongside each component (e.g., `SentimentTimeline.test.js` next to `SentimentTimeline.vue`). Use @vue/test-utils `mount` and `shallowMount`. For each visualization component test: 1) Renders without errors with valid mock data, 2) Renders an empty state message when data prop is empty or undefined, 3) Validates required props (test that missing required props trigger Vue warnings), 4) Emits expected events (e.g., 'drill' event on click), 5) Handles resize behavior (mock ResizeObserver). For D3-dependent components, note that D3 DOM manipulation works in jsdom but SVG rendering is limited — test that the expected SVG elements are created (querySelect for `<svg>`, `<circle>`, `<rect>`, `<path>`). Create shared mock data fixtures in `frontend/src/components/report/__tests__/fixtures.js`. Run tests with `pnpm test` (verify vitest is configured in the project).

tasks.title: Unit tests for new backend endpoints
tasks.description: Write pytest unit tests in `backend/tests/test_new_endpoints.py` for all new backend endpoints added in groups 0-2. Test the health detailed endpoint (`/api/health/detailed`) returns correct structure with all check fields. Test the seed generation endpoint (`/api/scenario/generate-seed`) with valid input returns seed text, with missing topic returns 422 validation error, and with no LLM key returns template text. Test token usage endpoints: `/api/settings/llm-usage` returns zeroed stats initially, and after mocking an LLM call, returns accumulated tokens. Test the sentiment endpoint returns per-agent data with scores in -1 to 1 range. Test the competitive endpoint returns mention counts for known competitors. Test the heatmap endpoint returns a matrix with correct dimensions. Test the influence endpoint returns a valid graph with nodes and edges. Mock time.time() for any time-dependent tests. Use pytest parametrize for testing multiple input variations.

tasks.title: Error boundary wrapper component
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.vue` using Vue 3's `onErrorCaptured` lifecycle hook. The component wraps its default slot content and catches any unhandled errors from child components. On error capture: render a friendly error UI instead of the broken component — show an icon (warning triangle), heading 'Something went wrong', the error message in small monospace text (collapsed by default, expandable), and a 'Try Again' button that resets the error state and re-renders the child. Log the full error details (message, stack, component name) to console.error. Add an optional `onError` prop callback for parent components that want to be notified. Wrap all major view components in App.vue or AppLayout.vue with ErrorBoundary: `<ErrorBoundary><RouterView /></ErrorBoundary>`. Also wrap individual high-risk components (D3 charts, external data views) with their own ErrorBoundary instances so a chart failure doesn't take down the entire page. Add a `fallback` named slot for custom error UI per usage.

tasks.title: D3 rendering performance monitoring
tasks.description: Add performance monitoring to D3 visualization components. Create a composable `frontend/src/composables/useChartPerformance.js` that wraps chart render functions with `performance.now()` timing. The composable exports `trackRender(chartName, renderFn)` which: records start time, calls the render function, records end time, logs render duration, counts DOM elements within the chart container (`container.querySelectorAll('*').length`), and stores metrics in a reactive Map. Log a console.warn when render exceeds 100ms or DOM node count exceeds 1000. In development mode (check `import.meta.env.DEV`), add a floating FPS counter overlay: create `frontend/src/components/dev/FPSCounter.vue` that uses `requestAnimationFrame` to measure frame rate, displaying as a small fixed badge in the bottom-left corner. Apply `useChartPerformance` to all D3 components: InfluenceGraph, EngagementHeatmap, SentimentTimeline, WordCloud, DecisionFunnel, ScatterPlot, HorizontalBarChart, ReportCharts. Conditionally render FPSCounter only when `import.meta.env.DEV` is true.

tasks.title: Interactive API docs page
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/views/ApiDocsView.vue` accessible at route `/api-docs` (add to router). Build an in-app API documentation page listing all backend endpoints. Define the endpoint catalog as a constant array with: method (GET/POST), path, description, request body schema (for POST endpoints — field names, types, required/optional), response example (JSON snippet), and a 'Try It' function. Render as a single-page layout: left sidebar with endpoint list grouped by category (Health, Scenarios, Graph, Simulation, Report, Chat, Settings), main area showing the selected endpoint's details. The 'Try It' button sends an actual request to the endpoint using the API client and displays the raw JSON response in a formatted code block (use `JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)` with syntax highlighting via CSS). For POST endpoints, show an editable JSON textarea for the request body. Style consistently with the rest of the app using brand tokens. Add a search filter at the top of the sidebar.

tasks.title: Architecture decision records
tasks.description: Create a `docs/adr/` directory in the project root with 5 Architecture Decision Record files following the standard ADR template (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences). Create: `001-vue3-framework.md` — why Vue 3 + Vite was chosen over React/Next.js (lightweight, fast dev server, Composition API for complex D3 integration), `002-flask-backend.md` — why Flask over FastAPI or Express (Python ecosystem for LLM libraries, lightweight demo server, rapid prototyping), `003-d3-visualizations.md` — why D3.js over Chart.js or Recharts (full control for custom force graphs and heatmaps, SVG export capability, animation control), `004-llm-integration.md` — multi-provider strategy with fallback to keyword matching (provider flexibility, demo works without API keys, cost management via provider switching), `005-demo-architecture.md` — why a mock demo backend instead of the full OASIS/camel-ai stack (5.8GB → 150MB image size, no GPU required, deterministic demo behavior, instant startup). Each ADR should be 200-400 words.

tasks.title: Presentation slides template (Marp)
tasks.description:
Create `docs/slides/demo-deck.md` as a Marp-compatible presentation. Add Marp frontmatter: `---
marp: true
theme: default
paginate: true
style: |
  section { font-family: system-ui; }
  h1 { color: #2068FF; }
---`. Design ~12 slides: 1) Title slide — 'MiroFish: Swarm Intelligence for GTM Strategy' with Intercom branding, 2) Problem statement — manual GTM campaign planning is slow and intuition-based, 3) Solution overview — AI agents simulate buyer behavior at scale, 4) Architecture diagram using Mermaid — show Vue frontend → Flask API → LLM providers → simulation engine, 5) How it works — 4-step flow (Scenario → Graph → Simulation → Report), 6) Knowledge Graph visualization (screenshot placeholder), 7) Simulation in action (screenshot placeholder), 8) Report insights sample, 9) Key metrics — simulated engagement, conversion, and sentiment data, 10) Demo walkthrough steps, 11) Roadmap — future enhancements, 12) Q&A slide. Include speaker notes using HTML comments. Add a 'How to present' section at the top of the file explaining how to use `npx @marp-team/marp-cli demo-deck.md --html` to generate HTML/PDF slides.

tasks.title: Updated README with screenshots
tasks.description: Rewrite README.md in the project root with comprehensive documentation. Include sections: **Overview** (2-3 paragraphs explaining MiroFish GTM Demo's purpose), **Features** (bulleted list with brief descriptions for each major feature: scenario builder, knowledge graph, simulation, reports, AI chat, agent profiles), **Architecture** (Mermaid diagram showing: `Vue 3 Frontend` → `Flask Backend` → `Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini`, with Zep as optional memory layer), **Quick Start** (both Docker: `docker compose up -d` and manual: separate backend + frontend commands with prerequisites), **Environment Variables** (table with columns: Variable, Required, Default, Description — list all env vars from the validation function), **Screenshots** (section with `![Landing Page](docs/screenshots/landing.png)` placeholders for: landing page, knowledge graph, simulation running, report view, chat interface), **API Reference** (table of all endpoints with method, path, and one-line description), **Project Structure** (tree diagram of key directories), and **Contributing** (brief guide for local development setup). Add badges at the top: license, Python version, Node version, Docker.

tasks.title: Troubleshooting FAQ
tasks.description: Create `docs/troubleshooting.md` with common issues and solutions organized by category. **Setup Issues:** 'API key not working' (check key format, provider matches key type, .env file is in project root not backend/), 'Docker build fails' (ensure Docker Desktop is running, check disk space, try `docker system prune`), 'Port already in use' (check for existing processes on 5001/3000, use BACKEND_PORT/FRONTEND_PORT env vars). **Runtime Issues:** 'CORS errors in browser console' (set ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var, check URL matches exactly including port), 'LLM responses not working' (check LLM_PROVIDER and LLM_API_KEY are both set, test with /api/health/detailed), 'Zep connection failed' (verify ZEP_API_KEY, check Zep Cloud status at status.getzep.com). **UI Issues:** 'Charts not rendering' (check browser console for D3 errors, ensure data endpoints return correctly), 'Simulation stuck at 0%' (check backend logs, try refreshing the page), 'Blank page after navigation' (clear localStorage, check for JS errors in console). Include troubleshooting commands and log locations.

tasks.title: GZIP compression middleware
tasks.description: Add response compression to the Flask backend. Install `flask-compress` via pip (add to the Dockerfile pip install line). In backend/demo_app.py, import and initialize: `from flask_compress import Compress` then `Compress(app)`. Configure compression settings: `app.config['COMPRESS_MIMETYPES']` should include 'application/json', 'text/html', 'text/css', 'text/javascript', 'application/javascript', 'image/svg+xml'.
