Data sources: 'revenue', 'pipeline', 'salesforce', 'cpq', 'orders', 'simulation', 'reconciliation'. Add factory methods for default dashboard configurations.

tasks.title: Create dashboard persistence API
tasks.description: Create backend/app/api/dashboards.py as a Flask Blueprint: GET /api/dashboards — list saved dashboards. POST /api/dashboards — create new dashboard from config. GET /api/dashboards/<id> — get dashboard with full widget and layout config. PUT /api/dashboards/<id> — update dashboard (name, widgets, layout). DELETE /api/dashboards/<id> — delete dashboard. POST /api/dashboards/<id>/duplicate — clone a dashboard. GET /api/dashboards/default — get the default dashboard config. Persist to JSON files in backend/data/dashboards/. Register Blueprint.

tasks.title: Build widget registry
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/WidgetRegistry.js — registry of all available widget types. Export a map of widget_type → { component, defaultConfig, icon, label, description, supportedDataSources }. Widget types: KpiCardWidget, LineChartWidget, BarChartWidget, DonutChartWidget, TableWidget, FunnelWidget, GaugeWidget, TextWidget, ActivityFeedWidget. Each widget component accepts standard props: { config, data, loading, error, onConfigChange }. Registry enables dynamic rendering: `<component :is="registry[widget.type].component" />`.

tasks.title: Build KPI card widget component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/widgets/KpiCardWidget.vue — configurable KPI card. Config: data_source, metric_name, label, prefix, suffix, show_trend, show_sparkline, color. Displays: large value, label, trend arrow (up/down with percentage), optional 6-period sparkline. Edit mode: form to configure all options. Preview mode: rendered card. Supports all data sources — fetches the configured metric from the appropriate store. Color options: auto (based on trend), or fixed brand color.

tasks.title: Build chart widgets (line, bar, donut)
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/widgets/LineChartWidget.vue, BarChartWidget.vue, DonutChartWidget.vue. Each uses D3.js v7 with consistent styling. Config: data_source, metrics (array), time_range, colors, show_legend, show_labels. Line chart: multiple lines, hover crosshair, legend. Bar chart: single or grouped bars, horizontal/vertical orientation. Donut chart: segments with labels, center text showing total. All charts: responsive to container size, animated on mount, configurable in edit mode. Edit mode: form with metric selector, time range, color pickers.

tasks.title: Build table and text widgets
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/widgets/TableWidget.vue and TextWidget.vue. Table widget: data_source, columns config (which fields to show), sort, pagination, row limit. Renders sortable table with configurable columns. Supports formatting (currency, date, percentage). Text widget: markdown content, font size, alignment. Useful for dashboard titles, notes, annotations. Edit with a simple markdown editor. Both widgets follow the standard widget props interface.

tasks.title: Build drag-and-drop grid layout
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/DashboardGrid.vue — drag-and-drop grid layout using CSS Grid. Grid: 12 columns, auto rows. Widgets placed by grid coordinates (x, y, width, height). Drag to reposition widgets. Resize by dragging edges/corners. Snap to grid. Show placement guidelines while dragging. Prevent overlap. Use a drag-and-drop library (vue-grid-layout or implement with native drag events). Install via pnpm. Edit mode: show grid lines, handles on widgets, add/remove buttons. View mode: clean layout without edit chrome.

tasks.title: Build widget picker panel
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/WidgetPicker.vue — panel for adding widgets to dashboard. Opens as a slide-over from the right side. Shows all available widget types from WidgetRegistry as cards with icon, label, description. Click to add widget to dashboard (placed in next available position). Category grouping: Charts, Cards, Tables, Other. Search filter. Preview of each widget type with sample data. After adding, auto-open widget configuration panel.

tasks.title: Build dashboard Pinia store
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/stores/dashboards.js — manages dashboard state. State: dashboards (array), activeDashboard (object), editMode (boolean), loading, error. Actions: fetchDashboards(), fetchDashboard(id), saveDashboard(config), deleteDashboard(id), setEditMode(bool), addWidget(widgetConfig), removeWidget(widgetId), updateWidgetConfig(widgetId, config), updateLayout(layoutItems), duplicateDashboard(id). Getters: widgetConfigs, layoutItems, isDirty (unsaved changes), widgetCount. API client: frontend/src/api/dashboards.js with matching functions.

tasks.title: Create Dashboard Builder view
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/views/DashboardBuilderView.vue — the dashboard builder page. Toolbar: dashboard name (editable), Save button, Edit/View toggle, Add Widget button, Dashboard selector dropdown, New Dashboard button, Delete button. Main area: DashboardGrid with all configured widgets. Edit mode: WidgetPicker slide-over, grid handles, widget config panels. View mode: clean presentation of the dashboard. Add route: { path: '/dashboards', name: 'Dashboards', component: DashboardBuilderView }. Add route: { path: '/dashboards/:id', name: 'DashboardDetail', component: DashboardBuilderView }. Nav item with layout-grid icon.

tasks.title: Build timeline scrubber composable
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/composables/useTimelineScrubber.js — Vue composable for synchronized timeline control. State: currentPosition (0-1 normalized), isPlaying, playbackSpeed, duration, marks (event positions). Methods: play(), pause(), togglePlay(), seek(position), stepForward(), stepBack(), setSpeed(multiplier). Events: onPositionChange(callback), onPlay(callback), onPause(callback). Provide/inject pattern so all child components can synchronize to the same timeline. Keyboard shortcuts: Space (play/pause), Arrow Left/Right (step), +/- (speed). Auto-calculate duration from data range.

tasks.title: Build timeline scrubber UI component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/timeline/TimelineScrubber.vue — the visual timeline control bar. Horizontal bar with: progress indicator, draggable playhead, event markers (colored dots at notable events). Below bar: timestamp labels, play/pause button, speed selector (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x), step buttons. Above bar: current time/round display. Hover over bar: tooltip showing timestamp at cursor position. Event markers: colored by type (decision=red, milestone=green, interaction=blue). Click marker to jump there. Smooth playhead animation during playback. Responsive to container width.

tasks.title: Build synchronized chart wrapper component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/timeline/SyncedChart.vue — wrapper that connects any chart to the timeline. Props: chartComponent (the actual chart), timeField (data field to sync on), highlightMode ('point'|'range'|'crosshair'). Renders the chart component and overlays a position indicator: - 'point': highlights the data point at current timeline position. - 'range': shades everything before current position. - 'crosshair': vertical line at current position. Uses the timeline composable inject to stay synced. Bidirectional: clicking on the chart also updates the timeline position.

tasks.title: Build timeline event markers component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/timeline/EventMarkers.vue — shows notable events on the timeline. Extracts events from simulation data: decisions, sentiment peaks/valleys, consensus moments, conflicts. Renders as clickable icons above the scrubber bar. Hover: tooltip with event details. Click: jump timeline to that position and show event detail panel. Category filtering: toggle which event types to show. Density indicator: when many events cluster, show a count badge instead of individual markers.

tasks.title: Build snapshot comparison component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/timeline/SnapshotComparison.vue — compare state at two timeline points. Two position pickers: 'Compare Point A at [time] with Point B at [time]'. Side-by-side view showing: metrics at point A vs point B, agent sentiments, network state. Diff highlighting: what changed between the two points (green=new, red=removed, yellow=changed). Summary statistics: total changes, biggest change, most affected agent. Preset comparisons: 'First vs Last', 'Before vs After Decision X'.

tasks.title: Build timeline annotations component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/timeline/TimelineAnnotations.vue — user-addable annotations on the timeline. Click any point on the timeline to add a note (stored locally in component state or Pinia). Annotations appear as small flag icons on the scrubber bar. Click to view/edit annotation text. Delete button per annotation. Export annotations as part of report data. Import annotations from shared configurations. Use for demo narration: pre-add annotations explaining what's happening at key moments.

tasks.title: Build multi-metric synchronized view component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/timeline/MultiMetricView.vue — stacked synchronized charts. Multiple chart rows, all synchronized to the same timeline scrubber: Row 1: Agent sentiment lines. Row 2: Interaction volume bars. Row 3: Consensus gauge. All rows share the same X-axis (handled by timeline scrubber). Crosshair line moves vertically across all rows simultaneously. Each row has its own Y-axis and can be individually toggled. Compact mode: sparkline versions. Expanded mode: full charts.

tasks.title: Integrate timeline scrubber into simulation workspace
tasks.description: Update frontend/src/views/SimulationWorkspaceView.vue to include the timeline scrubber. Add TimelineScrubber as a fixed bar at the bottom of the simulation workspace. Wrap existing simulation charts (sentiment, interactions, etc.) in SyncedChart wrappers. Add EventMarkers above the scrubber. In replay mode: timeline controls the replay position (replaces ReplayViewer transport controls). In live mode: timeline shows completed rounds and auto-advances. Add MultiMetricView as an alternative to individual chart panels. The timeline becomes the central navigation element for simulation data.

tasks.title: Build radar chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/RadarChart.vue — reusable D3.js radar/spider chart. Props: data (array of {label, values: {seriesName: value}}), maxValue, levels (grid circles), showLegend. Multiple overlapping polygons for comparing series (e.g., Agent A vs Agent B personality traits). Grid circles with value labels. Axis labels at each spoke. Tooltip on hover: axis name, all series values at that axis. Color per series from brand palette. Legend with toggleable series. Animated mount: polygons expand from center. Responsive to container size.

tasks.title: Build parallel coordinates chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/ParallelCoordinates.vue — D3.js parallel coordinates plot. Props: data (array of objects), dimensions (array of axis configs), colorBy (field name). Each vertical axis is a dimension. Lines connect data points across all dimensions. Line color based on a categorical field (e.g., segment, outcome). Brushable axes: drag to select range on any axis, filter lines accordingly. Axis reordering: drag axis labels to reorder. Highlighted line on hover with all other lines dimmed. Use for: comparing agents across multiple metrics, comparing accounts across dimensions.

tasks.title: Build chord diagram component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/ChordDiagram.vue — D3.js chord diagram. Props: matrix (NxN flow matrix), labels (array of names), colors (array). Arcs around the circle for each entity. Chords connecting entities with flow thickness. Hover arc: highlight all chords connected to that entity. Hover chord: show flow value. Tooltip with exact values. Legend if color-coded by type. Use for: agent-to-agent communication volume, data flow between systems. Animated mount: chords grow from zero. Responsive.

tasks.title: Build sunburst chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/SunburstChart.vue — D3.js hierarchical sunburst. Props: data (tree structure with value at leaves), colorScheme, centerLabel. Concentric rings showing hierarchy: inner ring = top level, outer = leaves. Arc size proportional to value. Click to zoom into a segment (becomes new center). Breadcrumb trail showing current zoom path. Back button to zoom out. Tooltip on hover: name, value, percentage of parent. Use for: account revenue by industry → plan → size, pipeline by stage → owner → product. Animated transitions when zooming.

tasks.title: Build streamgraph component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/StreamGraph.vue — D3.js streamgraph (stacked area with flowing shape). Props: data (time series with multiple categories), colorScheme, interpolation. Smooth flowing shape showing how category proportions change over time. Hover: highlight single stream, show value and percentage. Legend with toggleable categories. Click category to isolate. Use for: topic evolution over simulation rounds, revenue mix over time. Use d3.stackOffsetWiggle for aesthetic baseline. Animated mount.

tasks.title: Build bullet chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/BulletChart.vue — D3.js bullet chart for KPI targets. Props: metrics (array of {label, actual, target, ranges: [poor, ok, good]}). Each metric as a horizontal bar: gray range bands, black bar for actual, red marker for target. Compact format: multiple bullet charts stacked vertically. Use for: KPI target achievement (quota attainment, pipeline coverage, NRR target). Color actual bar: red if below poor threshold, yellow if in ok range, green if in good range. Responsive with proper label truncation.

tasks.title: Build calendar heatmap component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/CalendarHeatmap.vue — GitHub-style contribution calendar. Props: data (array of {date, value}), colorScheme, monthsToShow, tooltipFormatter. Grid of small squares: each day is a square. Color intensity by value. Month labels on top. Day-of-week labels on left. Hover: tooltip with date and value. Click: emit event with date for filtering. Use for: daily deal activity, daily sync job status, daily simulation runs. Color scale: white (no activity) to dark blue (high activity). Responsive: scroll horizontally if not enough width.

tasks.title: Build small multiples chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/charts/SmallMultiples.vue — D3.js small multiples layout. Props: data (array of datasets), chartType ('line'|'bar'|'area'), columns, sharedYAxis. Renders the same chart type for each dataset in a grid layout. All charts share the same scale when sharedYAxis=true for easy comparison. Individual chart hover shows value. Cross-chart hover highlights same X position across all charts. Use for: comparing metrics across agents, comparing funnel shapes across months. Grid layout adapts to number of datasets. Title for each small chart.

tasks.title: Build chart theme and utility library
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/lib/chartUtils.js — shared utilities for all D3 charts. Exports: brandColorScale (categorical), sentimentColorScale (diverging green-red), quantitativeColorScale (sequential blue), formatCurrency, formatPercentage, formatNumber, responsiveResize (handles window resize for D3 charts), createTooltip (standard tooltip creator), axisFormatter (consistent axis label formatting), chartMargins (standard margin convention). Brand palette: #2068FF (primary), #050505 (navy), #ff5600 (orange), #A0F (accent), plus 6 additional chart colors. All chart components should import from this shared library.

tasks.title: Create Charts Gallery view
tasks.description: Create a section in the existing VisualizationsShowcaseView.vue for the advanced chart gallery. Or extend the page to include: 'Advanced Charts' section with one example of each chart type: Radar (agent personality comparison), Parallel Coords (account multi-dimensional analysis), Chord (agent communication flow), Sunburst (revenue hierarchy), Stream (topic evolution), Bullet (KPI targets), Calendar (activity heatmap), Small Multiples (monthly comparisons). Each chart has a brief description and sample data. Interactive — users can explore. This serves as both a demo gallery and a component reference.

tasks.title: Create comparison data service
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/comparison_service.py — service for comparing simulations side by side. ComparisonService class: 1) `compare_simulations(sim_id_a, sim_id_b)` → structured comparison across all metrics. 2) `compare_agents(sim_id, agent_id_a, agent_id_b)` → agent-level comparison. 3) `compare_scenarios(scenario_type, sim_ids)` → compare multiple runs of same scenario. 4) `compute_statistical_significance(metric_a, metric_b)` → basic stat test for meaningful difference. Return ComparisonResult: {dimensions: [{name, sim_a_value, sim_b_value, difference, winner, significant}]}. Add API endpoint: GET /api/simulations/compare?ids=a,b

tasks.title: Build side-by-side comparison layout component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/comparison/ComparisonLayout.vue — split-screen comparison view. Left panel: Simulation A. Right panel: Simulation B. Header: simulation selectors (dropdowns), swap button, sync toggle. Sync toggle: when on, scrolling/interacting in one panel mirrors in the other. Divider bar: draggable to resize panels. Double-click to reset to 50/50. Each panel renders the same set of charts/metrics for its simulation. Difference overlay: toggle to show only where A and B differ.

tasks.title: Build comparison metrics table component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/comparison/ComparisonTable.vue — metrics comparison table. Columns: Metric, Simulation A, Simulation B, Difference, Winner, Statistical Significance. Rows: all comparable metrics (sentiment, consensus, decisions, agent engagement, etc.). Color-code winner column: A=blue, B=orange, Tie=gray. Significant differences highlighted with bold. Sortable by any column. Grouped by category (Engagement, Sentiment, Outcomes, etc.). Summary row: overall comparison score (how many metrics each simulation 'won').

tasks.title: Build comparative chart overlays
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/comparison/ChartOverlay.vue — overlay two datasets on one chart. Props: chartType ('line'|'bar'|'area'), dataA, dataB, labelA, labelB. Renders both datasets on the same axes with different colors (blue for A, orange for B). Toggle buttons: show A only, show B only, show both, show difference. 'Difference' mode: chart shows the delta between A and B values. Use for: overlaying sentiment trends, comparing pipeline progressions. Shared tooltip showing both values on hover. D3.js based.

tasks.title: Build A/B scenario builder component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/comparison/AbScenarioBuilder.vue — set up A/B comparison experiments. Select base scenario. Choose variable to change: LLM provider, agent count, personality distribution, round count, temperature setting. Configure A variant and B variant (the single changed parameter). Run both simulations in parallel with a 'Run A/B Test' button. Progress bar for each simulation. When both complete, auto-navigate to comparison view. Save A/B test configurations for repeatability.

tasks.title: Build comparison radar chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/comparison/ComparisonRadar.vue — radar chart showing two simulations. Dimensions: overall sentiment, consensus reached, decision quality, agent engagement, information spread, outcome satisfaction. Two overlapping polygons (blue=A, orange=B). Click dimension label to see detailed breakdown of that dimension's scoring. Summary text: 'Simulation A excels at X, while Simulation B performs better on Y.' Auto-generated from comparison service data.

tasks.title: Build comparison timeline component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/comparison/ComparisonTimeline.vue — synchronized dual timeline. Two stacked timelines (A on top, B on bottom) with shared X-axis. Events plotted on each timeline at the round they occurred. Visual links between similar events in A and B (e.g., both reached a decision at round 5 vs 7). Highlight divergence points: where outcomes differed. Shared scrubber control that moves both timelines together. Color-coded events matching the A/B color scheme.

tasks.title: Create Comparison view
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/views/ComparisonView.vue — the comparison analytics page. Layout: ComparisonLayout containing: Top: ComparisonTable showing overall metrics comparison. Middle: ComparisonRadar (left 1/3) + ChartOverlay (right 2/3) showing selected metric over time. Bottom: ComparisonTimeline (full width). Sidebar: AbScenarioBuilder for setting up new comparisons. Add route: { path: '/comparison', name: 'Comparison', component: ComparisonView }. Accessible from simulation results via 'Compare' button. Also direct nav access.

tasks.title: Build navigation mini-map component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/common/NavigationMiniMap.vue — a mini-map showing system overview. Small rectangular overview in bottom-right corner showing all GTM data dimensions. Dots/icons: revenue (green), pipeline (blue), orders (orange), accounts (purple). Dot size: proportional to metric magnitude. Dot brightness: recency of activity. Click a dot to navigate to that view. Current page highlighted with a box outline. Collapsible: click toggle to expand/collapse. Remember state in localStorage. Use SVG for crisp rendering at small sizes. z-index above other content.

tasks.title: Build global activity feed service
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/activity_feed.py — aggregates activity across all GTM domains. ActivityFeedService class: 1) `get_recent(limit, types, since)` → list of activity items. Activity types: deal_update, lead_scored, churn_risk, sync_complete, simulation_finished, report_generated, reconciliation_alert, order_provisioned, pipeline_milestone. Each activity: id, type, title, description, timestamp, severity (info|warning|critical), related_entity. Generate 50 realistic activities spread over last 24 hours using data from all generator services. Add API endpoint: GET /api/activity?limit=20&types=deal_update,churn_risk&since=ISO_DATE

tasks.title: Build global activity feed component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/common/GlobalActivityFeed.vue — real-time activity feed sidebar. Renders as a slide-out panel from the right edge, triggered by a bell icon in navbar. Each activity item: type icon, title, description, timestamp (relative), severity badge. Critical items: red background with pulsing dot. Warning: yellow highlight. Info: normal style. Category filter tabs: All, Deals, Leads, Risk, System. Unread count badge on the bell icon. Mark as read on view. Auto-refresh every 30 seconds. Smooth animations for new items. Maximum height with scrollable content.

tasks.title: Build notification toast system
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/common/NotificationToast.vue — toast notification system. Types: success (green), warning (yellow), error (red), info (blue). Appears in top-right corner. Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds (configurable). Click to dismiss. Optional action button (e.g., 'View Details'). Stack multiple toasts vertically. Animate in from right, fade out. Create frontend/src/composables/useNotifications.js composable: notify({title, message, type, duration, action}). Inject anywhere in the app. Update existing components to use this system for feedback messages.

tasks.title: Build breadcrumb navigation component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/common/Breadcrumbs.vue — hierarchical breadcrumb navigation. Auto-generates breadcrumbs from current route path and router meta fields. Example: Home > GTM Dashboard > Revenue > Account Detail. Last item is current page (not clickable). Others are links. Separator: chevron icon. Responsive: collapse middle items on mobile with '...' ellipsis. Add meta.breadcrumb to all routes in router/index.js. Place at top of main content area in the app layout.

tasks.title: Build quick search/command palette component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/common/CommandPalette.vue — Cmd+K style search palette. Opens with Ctrl/Cmd+K keyboard shortcut. Search across: page names (navigate), accounts (go to account), simulations, reports, metrics. Sections: Navigation (pages), Data (accounts, opportunities), Actions (create simulation, generate report). Fuzzy search with highlighting of matched characters. Keyboard navigation: arrow keys to select, Enter to execute, Escape to close. Recent searches shown by default. Popular actions pinned at top. Use overlay with semi-transparent backdrop. Smooth animation open/close.

tasks.title: Build system status bar component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/common/SystemStatusBar.vue — thin status bar at the very bottom of the app. Shows: current data mode (Full/Partial/Demo), active simulation count, last data refresh time, WebSocket connection status (green dot = connected), API response time (avg last 10 requests). Compact single-line format, 24px height, monospace font for data. Expandable on click: shows detailed system info (version, API endpoint, feature flags). Background color subtle: green tint for healthy, yellow for degraded, red for issues. Only visible in development mode or when user enables it in settings.

tasks.title: Integrate mini-map and activity feed into app layout
tasks.description: Update the main app layout (frontend/src/App.vue or layout component) to include: 1) NavigationMiniMap in bottom-right corner (persistent, collapsible). 2) GlobalActivityFeed triggered by bell icon in AppNav.vue (slide-out panel). 3) Breadcrumbs at top of main content area. 4) NotificationToast provider wrapping the app. 5) CommandPalette overlay (Cmd+K activated). 6) SystemStatusBar at the very bottom (toggle in settings). Ensure z-index ordering: palette > activity feed > toasts > mini-map > status bar. Update AppNav.vue: add bell icon with unread count, add Cmd+K hint in search area.

tasks.title: Create long-term memory consolidation service
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/memory_consolidation.py — consolidates agent memories across rounds. MemoryConsolidator class: 1) `consolidate(agent_id, memories)` — merges similar/redundant memories into consolidated facts. 2) `prioritize(memories, context)` — ranks memories by relevance to current context (recency, frequency, importance). 3) `forget(agent_id, threshold)` — remove low-priority memories (simulates forgetting curve). 4) `summarize_session(agent_id, session_id)` — creates a summary of key learnings from a simulation. Memory importance scoring: decisions=10, facts=7, opinions=5, observations=3. Recency decay: importance *= 0.9^(rounds_since_created). When Zep available, use Zep's built-in memory management. Otherwise, in-memory implementation.

tasks.title: Build memory-augmented agent prompts
tasks.description: Update backend/app/services/agent_prompts.py to incorporate long-term memory into agent context. New prompt section: 'Your memories from previous rounds: {consolidated_memories}'. Memory injection strategy: include top-K most relevant memories (default K=10). Relevance determined by: similarity to current discussion, recency, importance score. Add 'memory reflection' prompt: 'Based on your memories, your current beliefs are: {beliefs}. Your key knowledge is: {facts}. Your relationships: {relationships}.' Token budget: allocate max 30% of context window to memory injection. Truncate older/less relevant memories to fit within budget.

tasks.title: Build agent belief system tracker
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/belief_tracker.py — tracks how agent beliefs evolve over rounds. BeliefTracker class: 1) `extract_beliefs(agent_id, response)` — parse agent responses for belief statements. 2) `update_beliefs(agent_id, new_beliefs)` — merge new beliefs, handle contradictions. 3) `get_belief_history(agent_id, topic)` — timeline of belief changes on a topic. 4) `detect_belief_change(agent_id, round)` — identify when an agent changed their mind. Beliefs: key-value pairs like {'pipeline_outlook': 'optimistic', 'competitor_threat': 'high'}. Track confidence level per belief (0-1). Detect and flag contradictions. Store belief snapshots per round for temporal analysis.

tasks.title: Build agent relationship tracker
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/relationship_tracker.py — tracks inter-agent relationships over simulation. RelationshipTracker class: 1) `update_relationship(agent_a, agent_b, interaction)` — updates relationship based on interaction. 2) `get_relationship(agent_a, agent_b)` → {affinity: -1 to 1, agreement_rate, interaction_count, topics}. 3) `get_alliances()` → groups of agents who consistently agree. 4) `get_conflicts()` → pairs of agents who consistently disagree. Affinity calculation: +0.1 for agreement, -0.1 for disagreement, +0.05 for neutral interaction. Decay: affinity *= 0.95 per round without interaction (relationships fade without maintenance). Feed relationship data into agent prompts: 'You trust Agent X but disagree with Agent Y.'

tasks.title: Build memory search and retrieval component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/MemorySearch.vue — search through agent memories. Search input with: agent selector, query text, memory type filter (facts, beliefs, decisions). Results: memory cards with content, timestamp, importance score, source round. Highlight search terms in results. Sort by relevance or chronological. Click result to navigate to the round where this memory was formed. Show 'memory strength' indicator (bar) based on consolidated importance. Add 'memory map' visualization: word cloud of agent's most frequent memory topics.

tasks.title: Build belief evolution chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/BeliefEvolution.vue — tracks belief changes over time. X-axis: simulation rounds. Y-axis: belief topics. Each belief shown as a colored track: color represents the belief value (green=positive, red=negative, blue=neutral). Track width represents confidence level. Vertical markers where beliefs changed. Hover: see exact belief value and what triggered the change. Multi-agent comparison mode: overlay belief tracks from different agents. Summary: 'Agent changed their mind X times. Most volatile topic: Y.'

tasks.title: Build relationship network evolution animation
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/RelationshipEvolution.vue — animated relationship graph. D3.js force graph where edges represent relationships between agents. Edge color: green (positive affinity), red (negative), gray (neutral). Edge thickness: strength of relationship. Animation: play through simulation rounds, watch relationships form and change. Timeline scrubber integration. Pause at any round to examine state. Side panel: click an edge to see relationship history (timeline of interactions and affinity changes). Show alliance and conflict indicators as group outlines.

tasks.title: Create agent intelligence API endpoints
tasks.description: Add endpoints to backend/app/api/simulation.py: GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/beliefs — current beliefs with confidence. GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/beliefs/history — belief evolution timeline. GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/relationships — all relationships. GET /api/simulation/<id>/relationships — complete relationship graph. GET /api/simulation/<id>/alliances — detected alliances/coalitions. GET /api/simulation/<id>/conflicts — detected conflicts/disagreements. GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/memory/consolidated — consolidated memories. All endpoints return structured JSON suitable for direct visualization.

tasks.title: Create personality dynamics engine
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/personality_dynamics.py — models personality changes during simulation. PersonalityDynamics class: Personality is a vector of 5 traits (each 0-100): analytical, creative, assertive, empathetic, risk_tolerant. 1) `update_personality(agent_id, interaction_outcome)` — subtle personality shifts based on what happened. Positive consensus → slight increase in agreeableness. Rejected idea → slight decrease in assertiveness. Successful risk → increase risk tolerance. Failed prediction → increase analytical, decrease confidence. 2) `get_personality_snapshot(agent_id, round)` — personality vector at specific round. 3) `get_personality_trajectory(agent_id)` → full personality evolution. Changes are small (±1-3 per interaction) so personality is relatively stable but gradually evolves. Keep changes within bounds (20-80 range to prevent extreme personalities).

tasks.title: Create dynamic sentiment engine
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/sentiment_dynamics.py — models mood/sentiment changes during simulation. SentimentDynamics class: Agent sentiment is a floating value 1-10 (1=very negative, 10=very positive). 1) `update_sentiment(agent_id, events)` — shift sentiment based on round events. Good news/agreement → +0.5 to +1.0. Bad news/conflict → -0.5 to -1.0. Neutral interaction → slight regression to mean (5.0). 2) `get_group_mood()` → average sentiment across all agents. 3) `detect_mood_swings(agent_id)` → identify sudden large sentiment changes. Inject sentiment into agent prompts: 'Your current mood is {description}. This affects your tone.' Sentiment descriptions: 1-3='frustrated/pessimistic', 4-5='neutral/cautious', 6-7='engaged/optimistic', 8-10='enthusiastic/confident'.

tasks.title: Create personality-aware prompt modification
tasks.description: Update backend/app/services/agent_prompts.py to incorporate personality dynamics into agent behavior. Based on personality vector, modify system prompt: High analytical → 'You prefer data-driven arguments and ask for evidence.' High creative → 'You often suggest unconventional approaches and think outside the box.' High assertive → 'You speak confidently and advocate strongly for your positions.' High empathetic → 'You consider others feelings and seek win-win outcomes.' High risk tolerant → 'You are comfortable with uncertainty and favor bold moves.' Also inject current sentiment as mood modifier. Combine personality + sentiment + memories for a rich, evolving agent character.

tasks.title: Build personality radar chart evolution component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/PersonalityEvolution.vue — animated radar chart showing personality change. D3.js radar chart with 5 axes (analytical, creative, assertive, empathetic, risk_tolerant). Animated: polygon shape morphs over simulation rounds as personality evolves. Timeline scrubber controls the animation. Play to see personality shift. Color: start with blue outline, transition through stages to final purple. Show trait labels with current values. Change highlights: flash when a trait changes. Multi-agent comparison: show multiple agents' personality polygons overlapping.

tasks.title: Build sentiment arc visualization component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/SentimentArc.vue — narrative arc visualization of agent sentiments. D3.js area chart showing all agents' sentiments over simulation rounds. Each agent as a separate colored line with semi-transparent fill below. Annotate key events: decisions (vertical marker), conflicts (red zone), consensus (green zone). Show group mood average as a thick line. Highlight mood swing points. Interactive: hover to see exact sentiment values, click to see what happened that round. Story arc template overlay: setup → rising tension → climax → resolution (dashed reference line).

tasks.title: Build agent mood indicator component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/AgentMoodIndicator.vue — visual mood indicator per agent. Emoji-style face that changes expression based on sentiment: 1-2: angry/frustrated (red), 3-4: worried/skeptical (orange), 5-6: neutral/thoughtful (gray), 7-8: pleased/engaged (light green), 9-10: excited/enthusiastic (green). Animate transitions between states. Pulse effect on mood changes. Show as overlay on agent avatars in the simulation feed. Click to see sentiment history for that agent. Compact and large versions for different contexts.

tasks.title: Build personality comparison matrix component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/PersonalityMatrix.vue — matrix comparing agent personalities. Rows: agents. Columns: personality traits. Cell: colored bar showing trait value (0-100). Color scale: low=red, medium=yellow, high=green (or brand-specific scale). Sortable by any trait column. Show initial vs current personality side by side. Highlight biggest personality changes with a delta indicator. Click agent to see PersonalityEvolution animation for that agent. Click trait column to see distribution across all agents.

tasks.title: Create personality dynamics API endpoints
tasks.description: Add endpoints to backend/app/api/simulation.py: GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/personality — current personality vector. GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/personality/history — trait history per round. GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/sentiment/history — sentiment per round. GET /api/simulation/<id>/personality/comparison — all agents' personalities for comparison. GET /api/simulation/<id>/mood — group mood overview. GET /api/simulation/<id>/mood-swings — detected significant mood changes. All endpoints return structured data suitable for direct charting.

tasks.title: Create coalition detection algorithm
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/coalition_detector.py — identifies groups of aligned agents. CoalitionDetector class: 1) `detect_coalitions(simulation_id)` → list of Coalition objects. Coalition: {members, shared_positions, formation_round, strength, label}. Detection algorithm: cluster agents by agreement matrix (who agrees with whom most). Use agglomerative clustering: start with each agent alone, merge pairs with highest agreement rate. Threshold: agents in same coalition agree on >60% of discussion points. 2) `track_coalition_evolution(simulation_id)` → how coalitions formed/split over rounds. 3) `identify_swing_agents(simulation_id)` → agents who switched coalitions. 4) `compute_polarization_index()` → 0-1 measure of how divided the group is.

tasks.title: Create coalition labeling service
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/coalition_labeler.py — auto-generates descriptive labels for coalitions. CoalitionLabeler class: 1) `label_coalition(coalition)` → descriptive name based on shared positions. Use LLM (or template fallback) to generate labels: - Analyze shared positions/opinions of coalition members. - Generate label like 'Revenue Optimists', 'Conservative Growth', 'Product-First Advocates'. 2) `describe_coalition(coalition)` → longer description of what the coalition stands for. 3) `explain_split(coalition_a, coalition_b)` → what divides two coalitions. Template fallback labels: '{Department} Block', 'Pro-{topic} Group', '{Trait}-Leaning'.

tasks.title: Build coalition visualization component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/CoalitionView.vue — D3.js visualization of coalitions. Force-directed graph with nodes grouped into coalition clusters. Coalition boundaries: colored convex hull shapes (semi-transparent). Intra-coalition edges: thick, colored by coalition color. Inter-coalition edges: thin, gray, dashed. Coalition labels displayed above each group. Strength indicator: border opacity correlates with coalition strength. Swing agents: shown with pulsing animation on the border between coalitions. Click coalition to see member details, shared positions, and formation history.

tasks.title: Build coalition evolution timeline component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/CoalitionTimeline.vue — shows how coalitions form and change. D3.js alluvial/river plot: X-axis is simulation rounds, flows show agents moving between coalitions. Each flow stream colored by coalition. Streams merge (coalitions forming) and split (coalitions breaking). Width of stream proportional to number of members. Annotate key events: coalition formed (marker), agent switched (highlighted flow). Click any round to see the coalition state at that moment. Summary: number of coalition changes, most stable coalition, most dynamic agent.

tasks.title: Build polarization index gauge component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/PolarizationGauge.vue — shows group division level. Semicircular gauge: left (consensus, green), middle (moderate, yellow), right (polarized, red). Needle shows current polarization index (0-1). Below gauge: text description: 'Low polarization — group mostly agrees' to 'High polarization — deeply divided'. Sparkline showing polarization over simulation rounds. Click for breakdown: what topics are most divisive, which agent pairs are most opposed.

tasks.title: Build swing agent profile component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/SwingAgentProfile.vue — detailed view of agents who changed coalitions. Shows: agent name, role, personality, and their coalition journey (which coalitions they belonged to when). Timeline: coalition membership over rounds with color-coded periods. Influence analysis: did this agent influence others when they switched? Key moments: what messages/decisions preceded the coalition change. Link to the specific round messages showing the transition.

tasks.title: Build consensus tracking component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/ConsensusTracker.vue — tracks group consensus over time. D3.js line chart: consensus percentage per discussion topic over rounds. 100% = full agreement, 50% = evenly split, 0% = impossible in practice. Multiple topics tracked simultaneously as separate lines. Highlight 'consensus moments' when group reaches agreement on a topic. Show number of open (unresolved) topics vs resolved topics as a stacked bar alongside. Useful for determining when a simulation has 'resolved' its main questions.

tasks.title: Create coalition API endpoints
tasks.description: Add endpoints to backend/app/api/simulation.py: GET /api/simulation/<id>/coalitions — detected coalitions with labels. GET /api/simulation/<id>/coalitions/evolution — coalition changes over rounds. GET /api/simulation/<id>/coalitions/polarization — polarization index timeline. GET /api/simulation/<id>/coalitions/swing-agents — agents who changed coalitions. GET /api/simulation/<id>/consensus — consensus tracking per topic. GET /api/simulation/<id>/consensus/resolved — list of topics where consensus was reached. All endpoints include both round-level and summary data.

tasks.title: Create agent behavior prediction model
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/behavior_predictor.py — predicts what agents will do next. BehaviorPredictor class: 1) `predict_next_action(agent_id, context)` → predicted action with confidence. Use simple Bayesian model: P(action | personality, sentiment, history, topic). Action categories: agree, disagree, propose_new_idea, ask_question, make_decision, stay_silent. 2) `predict_consensus_round(simulation_id)` → estimated round when group reaches consensus. 3) `predict_outcome(simulation_id)` → predicted final state (who wins the argument, final sentiment). All predictions include confidence intervals. Training data: past simulation rounds within the same simulation. Simple implementation: frequency-based prediction from agent's past behavior patterns.

tasks.title: Create behavior anomaly detector
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/anomaly_detector.py — detects unexpected agent behaviors. AnomalyDetector class: 1) `detect_anomalies(simulation_id, round)` → list of unusual events. Anomaly types: sudden sentiment reversal (>3 points), unexpected agreement (hostile agents agreeing), leadership emergence (quiet agent suddenly influential), topic hijacking (agent changes subject dramatically). 2) `score_surprise(event)` → 0-1 surprise score based on how unexpected the event was. 3) `explain_anomaly(event)` → generates human-readable explanation. Use Z-score of agent behavior metrics to identify outliers. Flag anomalies in simulation data for visualization.

tasks.title: Build prediction dashboard component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/PredictionDashboard.vue — shows behavioral predictions. Per-agent cards: predicted next action with confidence bars. Consensus prediction: estimated round with confidence range (±2 rounds). Outcome prediction: pie chart of predicted outcomes with percentages. Track prediction accuracy: after each round, compare prediction to actual. Show accuracy score. Update predictions after each round completes. Color-coded confidence: green >80%, yellow 50-80%, red <50%.

tasks.title: Build anomaly highlight component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/AnomalyHighlights.vue — surfaces unexpected events. List of detected anomalies sorted by surprise score. Each anomaly: agent name, description, surprise score (bar), round number, explanation. High surprise anomalies have eye-catching styling (border glow, bold text). Click to jump to the round where the anomaly occurred. Summary: total anomalies, most surprising agent, trend (anomalies increasing or decreasing).

tasks.title: Build what-if prediction panel component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/WhatIfPanel.vue — interactive what-if predictions. User can modify hypothetical conditions: 'What if Agent X's sentiment was higher?' Sliders: adjust agent sentiments, personality traits, add/remove agents. 'Predict' button: runs prediction model with modified inputs. Shows predicted outcome vs actual outcome side by side. Useful for understanding which factors most influence simulation outcomes. Sensitivity display: bar chart showing which input change has biggest impact on outcome.

tasks.title: Build agent behavior patterns component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/BehaviorPatterns.vue — recurring behavior pattern analysis. D3.js visualization showing patterns like: - Agent A always responds to Agent B within 1 message. - Agent C tends to agree initially then push back in later rounds. - Agent D escalates when sentiment drops below 4. Render as a pattern timeline with repeating motifs highlighted. Summary table: agent, pattern description, frequency, consistency score.

tasks.title: Build influence flow chart component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/InfluenceFlow.vue — shows how influence propagates. D3.js directed graph with edge labels showing influence direction and strength. Node size: overall influence score. Edge arrows: who influences whom. Edge thickness: influence strength. Edge color: influence type (opinion change vs decision impact). Animate influence propagation over rounds. Identify 'influence chains': A influenced B who influenced C. Highlight key influencers and their reach (direct and indirect influence count).

tasks.title: Create prediction API endpoints
tasks.description: Add endpoints to backend/app/api/simulation.py: GET /api/simulation/<id>/predictions — current predictions for all agents. GET /api/simulation/<id>/predictions/accuracy — prediction accuracy scorecard. GET /api/simulation/<id>/anomalies — detected anomalies with surprise scores. POST /api/simulation/<id>/predictions/what-if — what-if prediction with modified inputs. Body: {modifications: [{agent_id, trait, new_value}]}. Returns predicted outcome. GET /api/simulation/<id>/patterns — detected recurring behavior patterns. GET /api/simulation/<id>/influence — influence flow graph data.

tasks.title: CHECKPOINT: Verify app builds and runs after agent intelligence features
tasks.description: Run comprehensive build and smoke test: 1) cd frontend && pnpm install && pnpm build — verify no build errors from new Vue components. 2) cd frontend && pnpm lint — verify no linting errors. 3) cd backend && pip install -r requirements.txt — verify all dependencies. 4) Test agent intelligence imports: python -c 'from app.services.personality_dynamics import PersonalityDynamics'. 5) Test coalition detection: python -c 'from app.services.coalition_detector import CoalitionDetector'. 6) Test behavior predictor: python -c 'from app.services.behavior_predictor import BehaviorPredictor'. 7) Verify all new API endpoints registered. 8) Start backend in demo mode and test: /api/simulation endpoints return mock data. 9) Verify no circular imports in the services layer. 10) Check total new Vue components count and route count. Fix any issues. Create docs/checkpoint-group-46.md.

tasks.title: Create agent reasoning parser
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/reasoning_parser.py — parses agent responses to extract reasoning steps. ReasoningParser class: 1) `parse_response(raw_response)` → {thought, message, decision, sentiment, reasoning_steps}. Reasoning steps: ordered list of {step_type, content, confidence}. Step types: observation, inference, evaluation, decision, justification. 2) `extract_logic_chain(reasoning_steps)` → simplified cause-and-effect chain. 3) `identify_assumptions(reasoning_steps)` → implicit assumptions the agent made. Handle different LLM output formats: Claude (XML tags), GPT (JSON), Gemini (structured). Graceful fallback: if reasoning can't be parsed, return raw response with 'unparsed' flag.

tasks.title: Create decision explanation generator
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/decision_explainer.py — generates human-readable explanations of agent decisions. DecisionExplainer class: 1) `explain_decision(agent_id, decision, context)` → explanation in plain English. Uses LLM (or template) to generate: 'Agent made this decision because... They considered X, Y, Z. The main factor was Z because it aligned with their personality trait of {trait}.' 2) `generate_counterfactual(decision, alternative)` → what would have happened differently. 3) `score_decision_quality(decision, outcome)` → retrospective quality score. Template fallback: fill-in-the-blank explanations based on decision type and agent traits.

tasks.title: Build reasoning trace component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/ReasoningTrace.vue — shows agent's thinking process. Expandable card that shows the full reasoning behind an agent's message. Sections: THOUGHT (internal reasoning, italic), OBSERVATION (what the agent noticed), INFERENCE (conclusions drawn), DECISION (action taken), JUSTIFICATION (why this action). Each section with an icon and collapsible. Show confidence indicator per step. Highlight assumptions with a subtle warning icon. Toggle between 'clean' view (just message) and 'transparent' view (full reasoning).

tasks.title: Build decision tree visualization component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/DecisionTree.vue — D3.js tree showing decision logic. Root: the question/topic. Branches: considerations/options. Leaves: chosen/rejected options. Green path: the agent's chosen path. Red paths: rejected alternatives. Edge labels: reasoning for branching. Node labels: consideration or option. Confidence percentages on each branch. Click node to see the agent's reasoning for that consideration. Collapsible branches for complex decisions.

tasks.title: Build argument map component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/ArgumentMap.vue — visual map of arguments for/against a decision. D3.js layout with central thesis node, supporting arguments (green) and opposing arguments (red). Each argument node: agent who made it, round number, strength score. Arguments can have sub-arguments (supporting/undermining the parent argument). Edge labels: relationship type (supports, undermines, rebuts). Build from parsing all agent messages related to a specific decision topic. Summary: argument strength balance, key turning points.

tasks.title: Build counterfactual comparison component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/simulation/Counterfactual.vue — shows what-if scenarios for decisions. Select a decision point in the simulation. Show: what happened (actual outcome) vs what could have happened (alternative). Side-by-side comparison with impact assessment: 'If Agent X had agreed instead of disagreed, the group would likely have reached consensus 2 rounds earlier.' Confidence estimate on counterfactual. Useful for understanding pivotal moments in the simulation.

tasks.title: Build transparency toggle for simulation messages
tasks.description: Update frontend/src/components/simulation/LiveFeed.vue (or SimulationPanel.vue) to add transparency mode. Toggle button: 'Show Agent Thinking'. When on, each message shows the full ReasoningTrace inline. When off, only the final MESSAGE is shown (clean view). In transparent mode: indent the THOUGHT/OBSERVATION sections, show them in a lighter font. Add 'Thinking...' indicator while agent is processing (between message events). Per-agent toggle: choose which agents to see thinking for. Useful for demo: show thinking for one key agent while keeping others clean.

tasks.title: Create reasoning transparency API endpoints
tasks.description: Add endpoints to backend/app/api/simulation.py: GET /api/simulation/<id>/round/<n>/reasoning — all agents' reasoning traces for a round. GET /api/simulation/<id>/agents/<agent_id>/reasoning — all reasoning traces for an agent. GET /api/simulation/<id>/decisions — list of all decisions with reasoning and explanations. GET /api/simulation/<id>/decisions/<decision_id>/explain — detailed explanation of a decision. GET /api/simulation/<id>/decisions/<decision_id>/counterfactual — counterfactual analysis. GET /api/simulation/<id>/argument-map/<topic> — argument map for a discussion topic.

tasks.title: Create custom agent configuration model
tasks.description: Create backend/app/models/custom_agent.py with dataclasses: CustomAgentConfig (id, name, role, department, personality: PersonalityVector, expertise_areas: list[str], communication_style: str, biases: list[str], goals: list[str], backstory: str, avatar_color: str, created_at). PersonalityVector (analytical, creative, assertive, empathetic, risk_tolerant — each 0-100). Communication styles: 'formal', 'casual', 'data_driven', 'storytelling', 'diplomatic'. tasks.title: Create debate orchestration engine
tasks.description: Create backend/app/services/debate_engine.py — structured debate mode for agent simulations. DebateEngine class: 1) `setup_debate(topic, agents, format)` — configures a structured debate. Formats: 'oxford' (for/against teams), 'panel' (moderated discussion), 'roundtable' (free discussion). 2) `run_opening_statements(agents)` — each agent presents initial position. 3) `run_rebuttal_round(agents)` — agents respond to others' positions. 4) `run_cross_examination(questioner, respondent)` — one agent questions another. 5) `run_closing_statements(agents)` — final positions after hearing all arguments. 6) `run_vote(agents)` → vote result on the debate topic. Oxford format: assign agents to 'For' and 'Against' teams. Moderator (optional) manages flow. Track argument quality, persuasion events, and opinion changes throughout.

Metrics: argument quality avg, rebuttal effectiveness, question quality, audience impact. 3) `determine_winner(debate)` → winning side/agent based on aggregate scoring. 4) `generate_scorecard(debate)` → formatted scorecard with per-agent breakdown. Use LLM for argument quality assessment (or simple heuristics as fallback).

Modification types: change_agent_count, change_agent_personality, change_environment, change_llm_provider, change_rounds, change_constraints. 2) `run_scenario(scenario_config)` → simulation results. 3) `compare_to_base(base_id, variant_id)` → structured comparison. 4) `run_sensitivity(base_id, parameter, range)` → run multiple variations of one parameter. Sensitivity score: how much the outcome metric changes per unit change in the parameter. 2) `rank_parameters(base_id, parameters)` → ranked list of most impactful parameters. 3) `generate_tornado_data(base_id, parameters)` → data for tornado chart (best/worst case per parameter). Parameters to analyze: agent count, round count, personality mix, temperature, initial conditions. Outcome metrics: consensus reached (bool), avg sentiment, decision quality, time to resolution.
